[G11B 15/60] Guiding record carrier -- G11B 15/60 covers mechanisms that guide or steer filamentary or web-type record carriers such as paper, magnetic tape or other media within information-storage devices. The classification addresses devices that control the path, alignment and movement of the carrier during driving, starting or stopping operations.
[G11B 15/602] for track selection, acquisition or following -- This CPC subclass covers methods and devices that select, acquire or follow a specific track on a moving record carrier such as a filamentary or web medium, controlling its motion for reading or writing. It falls under physics, information storage, relative movement between carrier and transducer.
[G11B 15/605] without displacing the guiding means -- The G11B 15/605 subclass deals with methods for driving, starting or stopping filamentary or web-type record carriers in information-storage devices where the guiding elements are kept stationary. It focuses on mechanisms that move the media relative to the transducer without displacing the guides, enabling precise control of the recording process.
[G11B 15/607] Pneumatic guiding -- Pneumatic guiding uses pressurised air to direct and control the movement of filamentary or web-type record carriers in data-storage systems, providing precise positioning for driving, starting or stopping the media during operation. This method improves handling accuracy and reduces mechanical wear in devices that store information by relative motion between the carrier and a transducer.
[G11B 15/61] on drum, e.g. drum containing rotating heads -- G11B 15/61 covers devices that drive, start, stop or otherwise control drum-type record carriers in which the drum itself contains rotating read/write heads, typically used in magnetic or optical information-storage systems.
[G11B 15/615] inside container -- The CPC subclass G11B 15/615 covers devices that drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type record carriers when they are located inside a container. It relates to information storage systems in which the media move relative to a transducer, such as film or magnetic tape handling mechanisms within sealed housings.
[G11B 15/62] Maintaining desired spacing between record carrier and head -- The class concerns mechanisms that keep a precise gap between a moving record carrier such as a filament or web and its read/write head, using control, feedback or spring systems to adjust the carrier's position during operation and maintain optimal spacing for reliable data recording.
[G11B 15/64] by fluid-dynamic spacing -- The G11B 15/64 subclass deals with driving, starting or stopping filamentary or web-type record carriers by using fluid-dynamic spacing, such as air or gas jets, to control their movement relative to the transducer. It is part of the information-storage field that relies on relative motion between carrier and read/write head.
[G11B 15/66] Threading -- This subclass deals with mechanisms that drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type record carriers by threading them onto rollers, guides or other transducer components in information-storage devices, covering methods for inserting, aligning or removing the carrier to control its motion for data recording or retrieval.
[G11B 15/662] Positioning or locking of spool or reel -- This subclass covers mechanisms that move or hold a spool or reel carrying a magnetic or optical web, controlling start, stop, or precise positioning for data recording or playback. It includes devices that lock the reel in place or adjust its tension to ensure accurate positioning of the record carrier relative to the transducer.
[G11B 15/665] by extracting loop of record carrier from container -- G11B 15/665 covers devices or methods that extract a loop of a filamentary record carrier, such as magnetic tape, from its container so the loop can be driven, started or stopped for recording or playback. It belongs to the physics subclass for information storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer.
[G11B 15/6651] to pull the record carrier against non rotating heads -- This subclass covers mechanisms that advance a filamentary or web-type record medium by pulling it past stationary read/write elements rather than using rotating heads. It relates to driving, starting or stopping such carriers in information-storage devices where the transducer remains fixed.
[G11B 15/6653] to pull the record carrier against drum -- G11B 15/6653 covers mechanisms that pull a filamentary or web-type record carrier so that it contacts a rotating drum, providing the driving force for recording, printing or similar processes. The classification includes devices that start, stop or control the movement of the carrier by tensioning it against the drum surface.
[G11B 15/6655] using one loading ring, i.e. "C-type"  G11B15/6658  takes precedence -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry G11B 15/6655 pertains to the method of driving, starting, or stopping filamentary or web form record carriers using a single loading ring, often referred to as "C-type." This classification is specific to the mechanics of loading and unloading processes in tape drives.
[G11B 15/6656] using two-sided extraction, i.e. "M-type" -- The G11B 15/6656 classification concerns methods that use a two-sided extraction, referred to as an M-type arrangement, to drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type record carriers in information-storage devices. This technique controls the relative motion between the carrier and the transducer for precise positioning.
[G11B 15/6658] with two loading rings rotating in opposite directions -- The G11B 15/6658 subclass covers devices that drive filamentary or web-type recording media by using two loading rings that rotate in opposite directions to feed, tension or stop the media during operation.
[G11B 15/67] by extracting end of record carrier from container or spool -- G11B 15/67 relates to methods of extracting the end of a filamentary or web-type record carrier from its container or spool, enabling the start or stop of the carrier's movement for information-storage operations.
[G11B 15/671] using pneumatic means -- G11B 15/671 covers information-storage devices that use pneumatic means to drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type record carriers, focusing on the control of their relative movement between carrier and transducer.
[G11B 15/672] Extracting end of record carrier from container or single reel  G11B15/671  takes precedence -- Subclass G11B 15/672 covers devices or steps that pull the free end of a filamentary or web-like record carrier out of its container or single reel to enable engagement with a transducer. It is part of the hierarchy dealing with driving, starting and stopping such carriers in information-storage systems. This subclass is subordinate to G11B 15/671, which has precedence.
[G11B 15/673] Threading end of record carrier externally to single reel  G11B15/671  takes precedence -- G11B 15/673 covers techniques for threading the free end of a filamentary or web-type record carrier onto a single reel from the outside, including mechanisms that drive, start or stop the carrier during the threading process. The broader subclass G11B 15/671 is considered prior and takes precedence.
[G11B 15/674] Threading or attaching end of record carrier on or to single reel  G11B15/671  takes precedence -- G11B 15/674 covers the method of threading or securing the end of a filamentary or web-type record carrier onto a single reel, a specific step in mechanisms that drive, start or stop such media in information-storage devices. It refines the broader class dealing with movement-based storage media handling.
[G11B 15/675] Guiding containers -- The subclass covers mechanisms that guide containers holding recording media such as filaments or webs, ensuring proper alignment and movement when the carriers are driven, started or stopped. It includes guiding systems for the transport and positioning of these containers within information-storage devices.
[G11B 15/67502] Details -- G11B 15/67502 pertains to specific details in the driving, starting, or stopping mechanisms for record carriers that are in filamentary or web form. This classification covers technical aspects and components involved in the precise control of such media during information storage processes.
[G11B 15/67505] Servo control -- The CPC entry G11B 15/67505 refers to servo-control techniques for driving, starting or stopping filamentary or web-type record carriers in information-storage devices, where the carrier's motion relative to a transducer is used to store data. It covers control systems that regulate the movement of these media to ensure accurate recording and playback.
[G11B 15/67507] Ejection damping means -- G11B 15/67507 covers ejection damping means that control the motion of filamentary or web-type record carriers when they are driven, started or stopped in information-storage devices. The feature provides shock absorption to prevent damage during rapid carrier ejection.
[G11B 15/6751] with movement of the cassette parallel to its main side, i.e. front loading  G11B15/67544  takes precedence -- Class G11B 15/6751 covers mechanisms that drive, start or stop filamentary or web record carriers when the cassette moves parallel to its main side, such as front-loading designs.
[G11B 15/67513] and movement of driving elements perpendicular thereto -- This subclass covers systems where driving elements move perpendicular to the direction of the record carrier, such as mechanisms that shift rollers or belts sideways to control filamentary or web-type media in storage devices. It pertains to methods of initiating, stopping, or steering the transport of the medium.
[G11B 15/67515] with servo control -- G11B 15/67515 covers mechanisms that drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type recording media using servo control. The servo system monitors the carrier's position or speed and adjusts motor action to keep motion precise for accurate data recording or retrieval.
[G11B 15/67518] with ejection damping means -- This subclass covers devices for driving, starting, or stopping filamentary or web record carriers that include means for damping the ejection of the carrier, reducing shock or vibration during discharge. It falls under information storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer.
[G11B 15/67521] of cassette with internal belt drive -- This CPC subclass covers cassette devices in which a flexible record carrier is moved by an internal belt mechanism, addressing the driving, starting, or stopping of filamentary or web-type media within the cassette housing. [G11B 15/67523] with servo control -- The subclass deals with information-storage devices that employ servo control to drive, start or stop filamentary or web-form record carriers, ensuring precise relative movement between the carrier and the transducer.
[G11B 15/67526] with ejection damping means -- This subclass addresses devices that drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type record carriers and include damping means for the carrier's ejection, limiting shock and vibration to protect the medium during release.
[G11B 15/67528] of endless tape cassette -- This subclass deals with devices that drive, start or stop an endless tape cassette, a type of filamentary recording medium. [G11B 15/67531] with servo control -- The G11B 15/67531 subclass covers devices that drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type record carriers using servo-controlled mechanisms, ensuring precise relative motion between the carrier and the transducer in information-storage systems.
[G11B 15/67534] with ejection damping means -- G11B 15/67534 covers devices that incorporate damping elements to control the ejection of filamentary or web-type record carriers, smoothing the motion when the carrier is started, driven, or stopped. [G11B 15/67544] with movement of the cassette parallel to its main side and subsequent movement perpendicular thereto, i.e. front loading -- The entry covers a front-loading method for information-storage devices where a filamentary or web-type record carrier, such as a cassette, is first moved parallel to its main side and then shifted perpendicular to it, enabling driving, starting or stopping of the carrier during operation.
[G11B 15/67547] the two movements being made by the cassette holder -- G11B 15/67547 relates to mechanisms that provide two separate movements of a cassette holder in information storage devices, such as initiating and stopping the transport of a filamentary or web record carrier relative to a transducer.

[G11B 15/67555] the second movement only being made by the cassette holder -- This subclass covers record-carrier systems where, after an initial drive, the second movement of the media is produced solely by the cassette holder, such as by tilting, rotating or shifting the cassette to advance or position the tape or web without any additional drive mechanism.

[G11B 15/683] wherein the recorder or player is moved according to the location of a selected cassette  G11B15/684  takes precedence -- G11B 15/683 pertains to mechanisms in which the recorder or player moves based on the position of a selected cassette, though it is noted that G11B 15/684 has precedence in classification. [G11B 15/684] the cassettes having a storage position inside the magazine and a slightly shifted active position, e.g. by solenoid -- G11B 15/684 covers mechanisms that hold a cassette in a storage slot within a magazine and shift it to a slightly offset active position for reading or writing, often using a solenoid or similar actuator. [G11B 15/686] with a fixed recorder or player in the centre or at the periphery of the magazine -- This CPC subclass covers devices that drive, start or stop filamentary or web-type record carriers when the recorder or player is fixed either at the centre or around the periphery of the magazine, defining the relative motion between the moving carrier and a stationary transducer.

[G11B 17/025] of discs which are stationary during transducing operation -- G11B 17/025 pertains to disc-type information carriers that stay fixed while the transducing element moves during reading or writing. [G11B 17/0255] flexible discs -- G11B 17/0255 covers flexible disc media used for storing information where the disc moves relative to a read/write transducer, and the disc is not a filament or web type but a pliable circular carrier.
[G11B 17/028] of discs rotating during transducing operation -- G11B 17/028 covers information-storage devices that employ disc-shaped record carriers which rotate during the read-or-write (transducing) process, such as magnetic or optical rotary media. The classification applies when the carrier is not a filamentary or web form and includes mechanisms that guide the rotating disc relative to the transducer.
[G11B 17/0281] by an adapter enabling the centre-pin to receive carriers with large centre hole -- This subclass covers devices that use an adapter to allow a centre-pin mechanism to accept record carriers with a larger central aperture than standard, facilitating guidance of non-filamentary carriers in storage systems where the carrier moves relative to the read/write transducer.
[G11B 17/0282] by means provided on the turntable -- G11B 17/0282 covers information-storage devices that use a turntable with mechanisms for moving the record carrier relative to the transducer, such as a rotating platter that positions the medium for reading or writing, and is applied to carriers that are not filamentary or web-type supports.
[G11B 17/0283] Two or more turntables -- G11B 17/0283 classifies devices that employ two or more turntables to store or retrieve information, using the relative motion between the rotating record carriers and a transducer. [G11B 17/0284] by clampers -- The subclass G11B 17/0284 covers information-storage devices that secure the record carrier by means of clamp-like holders rather than by filamentary or web supports. It pertains to systems where a clamping mechanism positions the medium against a transducer for reading or writing.
[G11B 17/0285] mounted on a bridge -- The CPC subclass G11B 17/0285 pertains to information storage devices where the recording medium is guided but is neither a filament nor a continuous web, and the medium is mounted on a bridge structure that supports its movement relative to the transducer.
[G11B 17/0286] mounted on a pivotal lever -- G11B 17/0286 covers information-storage devices in which the record carrier, which is not a filament or web, is mounted on a lever that pivots to bring it past a read/write transducer. [G11B 17/0287] by permanent connections, e.g. screws, rivets -- G11B 17/0287 covers devices that secure guiding record carriers-such as tapes or rollers that are not filamentary or web-type-by means of permanent mechanical connections like screws or rivets, enabling stable positioning relative to the transducer. This falls under information storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer within the broader physics field.
[G11B 17/0288] by means for moving the turntable or the clamper towards the disk -- G11B 17/0288 covers mechanisms that move the turntable or its clamping device toward a disk, such as actuators that position a rotating record carrier for engagement with a transducer. The subclass focuses on the means for advancing the disk or its holder rather than the transducer itself.
[G11B 17/03] in containers or trays -- G11B 17/03 relates to information-storage devices that hold the record carriers in containers or trays instead of using continuous webs or filaments, describing systems where each medium piece is individually supported and moved relative to a transducer for reading or writing. This falls under physics → information storage → storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer, specifically for non-filamentary, non-web carriers.
[G11B 17/032] Positioning by moving the door or the cover -- G11B 17/032 concerns storage devices in which the record carrier is positioned by moving a door or cover that controls access to the medium. The movement aligns the carrier with the transducer for reading or writing, typical of cartridge-type or removable-media systems where a protective door is slid or rotated to engage the medium.
[G11B 17/035] Positioning by moving the loading station -- G11B 17/035 covers storage systems where the loading station, rather than the medium, is moved to align the record carrier with the read/write transducer. This method is used for non-filamentary or non-web carriers such as disks or rigid media, allowing positioning by shifting the loading mechanism.
[G11B 17/038] Centering or locking of a plurality of discs in a single cartridge -- The G11B 17/038 subclass covers devices that centre or lock multiple magnetic or optical discs housed together in a single cartridge, ensuring correct radial alignment and preventing movement during handling or operation. Such mechanisms are used in multi-disc storage modules to maintain precise disc positioning relative to the read/write transducer.
[G11B 17/04] Feeding or guiding single record carrier to or from transducer unit -- The subclass covers devices that move or direct a single recording medium, such as a sheet, disc or cartridge, into or out of a read/write transducer. It includes feeding, positioning and guiding mechanisms that handle the carrier without using continuous webs or filamentary forms.
[G11B 17/0401] Details -- G11B 17/0401 pertains to the detailed aspects of guiding mechanisms for record carriers in information storage systems where the storage medium and transducer move relative to each other, excluding filamentary or web forms. This classification covers specific components and methods that ensure accurate tracking and positioning during data read or write operations.
[G11B 17/0402] Servo control -- G11B 17/0402 covers servo control mechanisms that position and regulate the movement of non-filamentary or non-web record carriers in information storage devices, ensuring precise relative motion between the carrier and the read/write transducer.
[G11B 17/0404] with parallel drive rollers -- This group covers information-storage devices that move a record carrier past a transducer using a set of parallel drive rollers, rather than belts or other drive mechanisms. It applies to rigid, sheet-like or non-filamentary carriers that are guided and propelled by the rollers.
[G11B 17/0405] Closing mechanism, e.g. door -- G11B 17/0405 relates to mechanisms that close or seal the housing of information-storage devices where the record carrier moves relative to the transducer, excluding filamentary or web carriers. It covers doors, latches and similar closing structures that protect the medium during operation or transport.
[G11B 17/0407] controlling the loading of the record carrier -- The class deals with methods and mechanisms for controlling how a record carrier is loaded onto a recording system, ensuring proper positioning and movement relative to the transducer. It applies to guided carriers that are not filamentary or web-type, such as discs or other supported media, and covers loading control in devices where the carrier moves relative to the read/write head.
[G11B 17/0408] of non-disc record carrier, e.g. card -- The CPC subclass G11B 17/0408 pertains to information-storage devices that employ a non-disc record carrier, such as a card, where data are recorded and read by relative movement between the carrier and a transducer. It excludes filamentary or web-type carriers and their supports, focusing on solid-state or card-based media used in magnetic, optical or similar storage technologies.
[G11B 17/041] specially adapted for discs contained within cartridges -- The CPC subclass G11B 17/041 concerns information-storage devices in which a disc is enclosed in a cartridge that is specially adapted for use with the carrier-transducer system, covering mechanisms for handling, loading or securing such cartridge-contained discs. It falls under physics, information storage, storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer, excluding filamentary or web carriers.
[G11B 17/043] Direct insertion, i.e. without external loading means -- G11B 17/043 concerns information storage systems in which the record carrier is placed into the device by direct insertion, i.e., the medium is introduced without the aid of external loading mechanisms such as rollers, belts or clamps. This subclass covers devices that guide carriers like discs or cartridges, rather than filamentary or web-type media, and emphasizes the simplicity of the loading operation.
[G11B 17/0432] adapted for discs of different sizes -- G11B 17/0432 covers apparatus or systems for storing information on discs that can be used with different diameters, where the drive or positioning mechanism is adjustable to accommodate size variations. It falls under physics-based information storage that relies on the relative motion between the disc carrier and the read/write transducer, excluding filamentary or web media.
[G11B 17/0434] with mechanism for subsequent vertical movement of the disc  G11B17/0438  takes precedence -- A subclass for information-storage devices where a disc record carrier includes a mechanism that moves the disc vertically after the transducer's operation, for positioning, ejection or loading. It belongs to the group on relative motion between carrier and transducer and is superseded by the more specific subclass G11B17/0438.
[G11B 17/0436] with opening mechanism of the cartridge shutter  G11B17/0438  takes precedence -- G11B 17/0436 pertains to guiding mechanisms for record carriers, specifically addressing those with an opening mechanism for a cartridge shutter. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO, focusing on information storage technologies involving relative movement between the carrier and transducer.
[G11B 17/0438] with mechanism for subsequent vertical movement of the disc and opening mechanism of the cartridge shutter -- The entry covers devices that store data on a disc-type record carrier and include a mechanism that lifts the disc vertically while operating a shutter to open the cartridge and expose the recording surface. It classifies the actuation system for vertical disc movement and cartridge-shutter opening in such information-storage media.
[G11B 17/044] Indirect insertion, i.e. with external loading means -- The subclass G11B 17/044 relates to information-storage devices where the record carrier is introduced into the mechanism by an indirect, externally applied loading system rather than by direct insertion. It covers the use of auxiliary loading means to position guided carriers such as plates, disks or other non-filamentary media within the storage path. This enables precise placement while the carrier moves relative to the transducer.
[G11B 17/046] with pivoting loading means -- This CPC class G11B 17/046 covers information-storage devices in which the record carrier moves relative to the transducer, is not of filamentary or web form, and employs a pivoting loading mechanism to engage the medium. It falls under the hierarchy PHYSICS → INFORMATION STORAGE → INFORMATION STORAGE BASED ON RELATIVE MOVEMENT BETWEEN RECORD CARRIER AND TRANSDUCER.
[G11B 17/0463] adapted for discs of different sizes -- G11B 17/0463 pertains to information-storage devices that use relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer, focusing on guide mechanisms that are neither filamentary nor web-type and are adapted to handle discs of different sizes.
[G11B 17/0466] with opening mechanism of the cartridge shutter -- G11B 17/0466 covers information-storage cartridges that include a mechanism for opening a shutter to expose the record carrier to the transducer. The classification applies to non-filamentary, non-web carriers such as magnetic or optical media where the cartridge's shutter is moved by a built-in opening device.
[G11B 17/047] with sliding loading means -- This subclass covers storage devices that use a sliding loading mechanism to insert or move a record carrier that is not a filament or web, such as cartridges or cassettes where the medium is guided and loaded by a linear sliding motion rather than by winding or feeding.
[G11B 17/0473] adapted for discs of different sizes -- The G11B 17/0473 subclass covers recording or reproducing apparatus that can be adjusted or configured to operate with discs of different diameters, rather than being restricted to a single size. It applies to systems where the record carrier is not a filament, web, or its support, and where information is stored by relative movement between the carrier and a transducer.
[G11B 17/0476] with opening mechanism of the cartridge shutter -- G11B 17/0476 covers cartridge-type information storage devices in which the record carrier is accessed by a moving transducer and the cartridge includes a mechanism that opens a shutter to expose the medium. It applies to storage media that are not filamentary or web forms, such as removable tape or disk cartridges with a protective shutter that is opened during operation.
[G11B 17/049] Insertion of discs having to be extracted from the cartridge prior to recording or reproducing -- G11B 17/049 covers devices where discs must be removed from a cartridge before recording or playback, in storage systems that use relative motion between a record-carrier and a transducer, excluding filamentary or web carriers.
[G11B 17/05] specially adapted for discs not contained within cartridges -- G11B 17/05 refers to information-storage devices in which a disc is read or written by relative movement between the disc and a transducer, and the disc is not housed inside a cartridge. It covers specially adapted systems for handling bare discs, distinct from filamentary, web-type, or cartridge-based media.
[G11B 17/051] Direct insertion, i.e. without external loading means -- G11B 17/051 relates to information storage systems where the record carrier is introduced into the device by direct insertion rather than by external loading apparatus. The carrier is guided but is not a filamentary or web-type element, such as a cartridge or disc that is pushed or placed manually into the mechanism.
[G11B 17/0515] adapted for discs of different sizes -- G11B 17/0515 is a CPC subgroup for information-storage devices that move a record carrier relative to a transducer, where the carrier is not a filament or web but a disc; the subclass covers designs that can accommodate discs of varying diameters.
[G11B 17/053] Indirect insertion, i.e. with external loading means -- G11B 17/053 refers to indirect insertion methods. This involves using external loading means to guide record carriers. It is a subclass of information storage based on relative movement.
[G11B 17/054] with pivoting loading means -- G11B 17/054 covers information-storage devices where a non-filamentary or non-web record carrier is guided into the transducer by a pivoting loading mechanism, such as a rotating or swinging arm that positions the medium for reading or writing. This subclass deals with arrangements that use a pivoted support to load the carrier onto the storage system.
[G11B 17/0545] adapted for discs of different sizes -- This subclass covers information-storage devices where the transducer moves relative to a rotating record carrier and the mechanism is designed to accommodate discs of varying diameters rather than a single fixed size. It applies to systems that guide non-filamentary or non-web carriers such as interchangeable discs.
[G11B 17/056] with sliding loading means -- G11B 17/056 pertains to storage systems in which a non-filamentary record carrier is guided and introduced to the transducer by a sliding loading mechanism, such as a tray or carriage that moves linearly to position the medium for recording or playback.
[G11B 17/0565] adapted for discs of different sizes -- The G11B 17/0565 subclass covers devices that guide record carriers of varying diameters, such as disc drives equipped with adjustable supports, clamps or positioning mechanisms that accommodate discs of different sizes. It addresses the adaptation of the guiding system to maintain proper alignment and relative motion between the disc and transducer when the carrier is not a fixed-size filament or web.
[G11B 17/057] specially adapted for handling both discs contained within cartridges and discs not contained within cartridges -- G11B 17/057 relates to devices specially adapted for handling both discs that are contained within cartridges and discs that are not, situated in the physics information-storage subclass dealing with relative movement between a record carrier and its transducer.
[G11B 17/08] from consecutive-access magazine of disc records -- The entry covers a magazine or cartridge that holds disc-shaped recording media and enables their sequential, or consecutive-access, retrieval. It relates to systems where the disc carriers are guided for reading or writing by a transducer moving relative to the record, rather than by filamentary or web-type media. This classification is used for devices that organize and feed disc records from a storage magazine.
[G11B 17/10] with horizontal transfer to the turntable from a stack arranged with a vertical axis -- G11B 17/10 pertains to systems where record carriers are horizontally transferred to a turntable from a vertically aligned stack, facilitating automated or manual loading in information storage devices. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European and United States patent offices.
[G11B 17/12] with axial transfer to the turntable from a stack with a vertical axis -- The G11B 17/12 subclass covers information-storage devices where the record carriers are neither filamentary nor web-type and are not on dedicated supports, focusing on systems that move the carriers axially from a vertically stacked pile onto a turntable for reading or writing. It is part of the broader physics field concerning storage based on relative motion between the carrier and transducer.
[G11B 17/14] by mechanism in rotating centre post, e.g. permitting the playing of both sides of a record -- G11B 17/14 covers information-storage devices where a rotating centre post or spindle is used to turn the record carrier so that both sides can be accessed by the transducer. The classification applies to mechanisms that move the carrier relative to the read-write head, excluding filamentary or web-type supports.
[G11B 17/16] by mechanism in stationary centre post, e.g. with stepped post, using fingers on post -- The class covers information-storage devices in which the record carrier moves relative to the transducer by means of a stationary centre post, often formed with stepped sections, and the motion is driven by finger-like elements that engage the post to advance or position the carrier.
[G11B 17/162] with means for detecting the diameter of the record -- A G11B 17/162 device stores information on a movable carrier such as a cylinder or disc that is not a filament or web, and includes a sensor that measures the carrier's diameter to aid processing or positioning. This feature helps maintain accurate reading or writing across the record's surface.
[G11B 17/165] with mechanical detecting means -- G11B 17/165 relates to information-storage devices in which a record carrier moves relative to a transducer and is guided by structures that are not filamentary or web-type, such as rollers or tracks, and where the data is detected by mechanical means such as pressure heads, contact stylus or other tactile sensors.
[G11B 17/167] with optical detecting means -- The subclass G11B 17/167 covers information-storage devices in which a record carrier moves relative to a transducer, but the carrier is neither filamentary nor a continuous web and is not mounted on a special support. It specifically pertains to systems that employ optical detecting means to read or write data.
[G11B 17/18] by mechanism operating on the edge of the disc record -- The entry G11B 17/18 covers recording or reproducing devices that read or write data by acting on the outer edge of a disc-type record carrier rather than on its face. It applies to edge-recording mechanisms for media that move relative to the transducer, such as certain magnetic or optical discs, where the carrier is not a filament or web.
[G11B 17/20] with transfer away from stack on turntable after playing -- G11B 17/20 covers mechanisms that move a disc-type record away from the turntable after playback, such as automatic ejection or transfer systems that retrieve the carrier from a stacked set. It falls under information storage based on relative motion between the record carrier and transducer, focusing on guiding carriers that are not filamentary or web-like. This subclass addresses the handling and removal of the record post-play.
[G11B 17/22] from random access magazine of disc records -- G11B 17/22 covers systems that retrieve or record data from a random-access magazine containing disc-type records, such as a carousel or stacked set of disks that move relative to a transducer. It falls under information storage based on relative movement between the record carrier and the transducer, specifically for carriers that are not filamentary or web-like.
[G11B 17/221] with movable magazine  G11B17/24  -  G11B17/28  take precedence -- G11B 17/221 pertains to guiding record carriers in information storage systems, specifically addressing configurations that involve a movable magazine. This classification is part of the broader Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) system used by the European Patent Office (EPO) and the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO).
[G11B 17/223] in a vertical direction -- G11B 17/223 covers information-storage devices where a non-filamentary, non-web record carrier is moved relative to a transducer in a vertical direction, allowing data to be written or read by the upward or downward motion of the medium.
[G11B 17/225] wherein the disks are transferred from a fixed magazine to a fixed playing unit using a moving carriage -- This CPC subclass G11B 17/225 covers mechanisms that transfer disks from a fixed magazine to a fixed playing unit by means of a moving carriage, defining handling of record carriers that are not filamentary or web forms.
[G11B 17/226] the magazine consisting of a single rotatable tray carrying the disks -- G11B 17/226 classifies a storage device where a single rotatable tray serves as a magazine that holds multiple disks, allowing the disks to be positioned relative to the read/write transducer without using filamentary or web-type carriers.
[G11B 17/228] Control systems for magazines  G11B17/225  takes precedence -- G11B 17/228 covers control systems for magazines used in information-storage devices that rely on relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer, excluding filamentary or web carriers. It addresses mechanisms that manage loading, positioning and selection of the media within the device and follows G11B 17/225.
[G11B 17/24] the magazine having a toroidal or part-toroidal shape -- The entry G11B 17/24 covers magazines or carriers for data storage media that have a toroidal or partially toroidal shape, used to guide record carriers that are not filamentary or web-like, such as disks or tapes wound in a donut-shaped format.
[G11B 17/26] the magazine having a cylindrical shape with vertical axis -- The code G11B 17/26 covers cylindrical magazines whose axis is vertical, used as record carriers in storage devices where information is read or written by relative movement between the carrier and a transducer, and which are not of filamentary or web form.
[G11B 17/28] the magazine having a cylindrical shape with horizontal axis -- This subclass covers cylindrical magazines oriented horizontally, used to feed or guide non-filamentary, non-web record carriers such as discs or rings in devices where storage is based on relative motion between the carrier and a transducer. It falls under G11B 17/00 for guiding record carriers and specifies a horizontal-axis cylindrical magazine.
[G11B 17/30] wherein the playing unit is moved according to the location of the selected record -- This subclass deals with storage devices where the playback or reading element is physically moved to the position of the chosen track or segment on a medium such as a disc or tape, rather than moving the medium itself. It applies to systems where the record carrier is not a filament or web and the transducer is positioned according to the selected record location.
[G11B 17/32] Maintaining desired spacing between record carrier and head, e.g. by fluid-dynamic spacing -- This subclass covers techniques for keeping a precise gap between a moving record carrier, such as a disc or drum, and the transducer head, often by using air or other fluid flow to control the spacing. It applies to storage devices where the medium is not a filamentary or web form and the spacing is actively maintained during operation.
[G11B 17/34] Guiding record carriers during transducing operation, e.g. for track following -- G11B 17/34 covers mechanisms that guide a record carrier while it is being transduced, such as systems that keep the carrier aligned with a magnetic or optical read/write head for accurate track following during recording or playback. It applies to carriers that are not filamentary, web-like, or supported on special substrates.
[G11B 19/00] Driving, starting, stopping record carriers not specifically of filamentary or web form, or of supports therefor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Any aspect of control regarding recording and reproducing devices which use carriers moving with respect to the transducer but which are not of filamentary (wire) or web (tape) form. This includes disks and drums, but is predominantly to do with disks.  Any form of control whether externally generated (e.g. user control, external shock) or internally (e.g. a response generated by the sensing of a feature of the record carrier).  Driving, starting and stopping such carriers, including details of control systems used for starting, stopping or altering the speed of motion of the carrier and details of the electromechanical arrangements used in driving, starting, speed-changing and stopping.
[G11B 19/02] Control of operating function, e.g. switching from recording to reproducing -- G11B 19/02 concerns control mechanisms that initiate, stop or switch the motion of a record carrier that is not a filament or web, such as drum- or disc-based storage, enabling functions like changing from recording to playback mode. It falls under physics-based information storage where data is accessed by relative movement between the carrier and transducer.
[G11B 19/022] Control panels -- G11B 19/022 relates to control panels that start, drive or stop record carriers in information-storage devices where the carrier moves relative to a transducer, excluding filamentary or web-type supports. It covers the user-interface hardware for initiating, regulating or terminating the motion of such carriers. This subclass falls under physics → information storage → relative-movement storage.
[G11B 19/025] 'Virtual' control panels, e.g. Graphical User Interface [GUI] -- G11B 19/025 classifies virtual control panels, such as graphical user interfaces, that let users manipulate recording devices through on-screen selections rather than physical knobs or buttons, applying to systems where a record carrier moves relative to a transducer.
[G11B 19/027] Remotely controlled  remote control systems in general  G08C -- G11B 19/027 pertains to remotely controlled systems for driving, starting, or stopping record carriers that are not specifically filamentary or web form, encompassing general remote control mechanisms as detailed under G08C.
[G11B 19/04] Arrangements for preventing, inhibiting, or warning against double recording on the same blank or against other recording or reproducing malfunctions -- This subclass covers mechanisms that prevent a second imprint on the same blank medium or that alert the operator to such errors, including sensors, interlocks and control circuits that detect and inhibit duplicate recordings.
[G11B 19/041] Detection or prevention of read or write errors -- G11B 19/041 covers methods and devices for detecting or preventing errors during reading or writing of information in storage systems where the medium moves relative to the transducer, such as magnetic or optical drives. It addresses error detection, correction, and control mechanisms that ensure data integrity in non-filamentary or non-web record carriers.
[G11B 19/042] due to external shock or vibration -- This subclass covers devices or methods where the movement of a record carrier in an information-storage system is initiated or halted because of external shock or vibration rather than internal mechanisms. It applies to non-filamentary carriers such as discs or drums where an impact causes the carrier to start, stop, or change speed.
[G11B 19/043] by detecting a free-fall condition -- G11B 19/043 pertains to methods for initiating, controlling, or halting the movement of storage media or their supports in devices where data is stored and retrieved through relative motion. This specific code addresses techniques that activate protective mechanisms by sensing when the device enters a free-fall state, aiming to prevent damage during unexpected drops.
[G11B 19/044] by using a data buffer -- The subclass covers techniques for driving, starting or stopping record carriers that are not filamentary or web forms by employing a data buffer, which temporarily stores information to coordinate motion control and timing of the carrier relative to the transducer. This enables precise start-stop actions without continuous mechanical linkage.
[G11B 19/045] by detecting mistracking -- This CPC subclass covers methods for detecting mistracking - misalignment between the moving record carrier and the transducer - in information storage devices that rely on relative motion, such as magnetic tape or drum systems. It falls under the physics area of information storage based on relative movement.
[G11B 19/046] Detection or prevention or problems due to temperature -- The G11B 19/046 entry covers techniques for detecting or preventing temperature-related problems in devices that drive, start or stop non-filamentary or non-web record carriers, such as rotating drums or discs, used in information storage based on relative movement between the carrier and a transducer.
[G11B 19/047] Recovery from power failure -- The G11B 19/047 class covers devices and procedures that enable a recording system whose carrier is moved by a motor or other drive to resume operation after a power interruption, including mechanisms for detecting the loss of power, retaining or restoring the carrier's position, and restarting the drive so that recording or playback can continue without data loss.
[G11B 19/048] Testing of disk drives, e.g. to detect defects or prevent sudden failure -- G11B 19/048 pertains to methods and apparatus for testing magnetic disk drives, such as techniques to detect defects, evaluate performance, and prevent sudden failures during operation. It falls under physics-based information storage where a record carrier moves relative to a transducer, specifically addressing driving, starting and stopping of non-filamentary carriers.
[G11B 19/06] by counting or timing of machine operations -- G11B 19/06 covers information-storage systems that control the movement of a record carrier-such as a disk or tape-by counting or timing the machine's operations, rather than using filamentary or web-type media, to start, stop, or drive the carrier. This subclass falls under the physics field of information storage based on relative motion between carrier and transducer.
[G11B 19/08] by using devices external to the driving mechanisms, e.g. coin-freed switch -- This subclass deals with driving, starting or stopping record carriers in information-storage devices by means of mechanisms that are external to the carrier's own drive, such as a coin-freed switch, rather than filamentary or web-type drives. It is indexed under CPC code G11B 19/08.
[G11B 19/10] by sensing presence or absence of record in accessible stored position or on turntable -- This CPC class G11B 19/10 covers mechanisms that detect whether a record such as a disc or other media is present in a stored location or on a turntable, using sensors that sense presence or absence. It pertains to systems that drive, start, or stop record carriers where detection is based on the record's presence rather than filamentary or web forms.
[G11B 19/12] by sensing distinguishing features of records, e.g. diameter -- G11B 19/12 covers mechanisms that drive, start or stop record carriers-excluding filamentary or web forms-by detecting distinguishing features of the records, such as their diameter, to control motion. It pertains to information storage devices that use sensor-based feedback to manage carrier movement.
[G11B 19/122] involving the detection of an identification or authentication mark  record carriers indicating unauthorised or prior use  G11B23/28 -- G11B 19/122 pertains to systems that detect identification or authentication marks on moving record carriers to indicate unauthorized or prior use, and includes mechanisms for driving, starting and stopping such carriers that are not filamentary or web-type. The classification is used for security-related information-storage devices.
[G11B 19/124] involving the detection of diameter of disks  feeding or guiding of a single record carrier  G11B17/04  and subgroups -- The CPC subclass G11B 19/124 covers devices that drive, start or stop a single record carrier, such as a disk, where the system detects the carrier's diameter while feeding or guiding it. It falls under information storage based on relative movement between the carrier and transducer, and is a narrower element of G11B 17/04. This code addresses control mechanisms that adjust motion according to measured disk size.
[G11B 19/125] involving the detection of carrier data format -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus that detect the data format encoded on moving record carriers such as disks or drums, focusing on identifying the carrier's format rather than its content. It relates to the control of motion for non-filamentary carriers and the recognition of their format for proper processing.
[G11B 19/127] involving detection of the number of sides, e.g. single or double, or layers, e.g. for multiple recording or reproducing layers -- G11B 19/127 covers mechanisms that drive, start or stop record carriers other than filament or web types, with particular attention to detecting how many sides or layers the carrier possesses. The detection is used for single- or double-sided and multiple-layer recording or reproducing applications.
[G11B 19/128] involving the detection of track pitch or recording density -- The G11B 19/128 subclass covers technologies that detect the track pitch or recording density of information-storage media, using the relative motion between the record carrier and the transducer. It includes methods and devices that measure spacing between adjacent tracks or evaluate how densely data are recorded, without being limited to filamentary or web-type carriers. This enables precise control of drive, start-stop and positioning functions in storage systems.
[G11B 19/14] by sensing movement or position of head, e.g. means moving in correspondence with head movements -- G11B 19/14 covers devices that detect the movement or position of a recording head by using elements that move in direct correspondence with the head, enabling control of driving, starting or stopping of record carriers in information-storage systems.
[G11B 19/16] Manual control -- G11B 19/16 refers to manual control of record carriers that are driven, started or stopped without using filamentary or web forms. It falls under physics, information storage, information storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer. The code covers manual mechanisms for operating such storage devices.
[G11B 19/165] by closing the cover -- G11B 19/165 covers information-storage devices in which the record carrier is driven, started or stopped by the action of closing a cover, rather than by filamentary or web mechanisms. It applies to systems where the cover's movement directly controls carrier motion.
[G11B 19/18] Manual action on one element producing control effect indirectly by consequent action of driving mechanism -- The CPC subclass G11B 19/18 covers manual manipulation of a single component that indirectly controls a record carrier by activating a driving mechanism. It pertains to systems that start, stop, or drive carriers-such as disks or drums-through a secondary motion rather than direct filamentary or web-type movement.
[G11B 19/20] Driving -- The CPC subclass G11B 19/20 covers mechanisms that drive, start and stop record carriers used for information storage when the carriers are not filamentary or web-type, such as rotating drums, discs or cylinders. It includes motors, clutches, gearing and other actuation devices that control the movement of these carriers.
[G11B 19/2009] Turntables, hubs and motors for disk drives; Mounting of motors in the drive  means for clamping of disk to turntable  G11B17/022  and subgroups -- The CPC entry G11B 19/2009 falls under physics → information storage → storage based on relative movement, and concerns the drive mechanisms for disk-based record carriers. It includes turntables, hubs and motors used in disk drives, methods of mounting those motors in the drive, and clamping devices that secure the disk to the turntable. This subgroup addresses mechanical control of rotation rather than filamentary or web media.
[G11B 19/2018] Incorporating means for passive damping of vibration, either in the turntable, motor or mounting -- The class covers record-storage devices that integrate passive vibration-damping elements into the turntable, motor or mounting assembly, reducing unwanted oscillations of the carrier during operation. Such measures improve playback stability for systems where the carrier and transducer move relative to each other without using active control.
[G11B 19/2027] Turntables or rotors incorporating balancing means; Means for detecting imbalance -- This subclass covers turntables or rotors used in data-storage devices that include built-in balancing mechanisms and sensors for detecting imbalance, ensuring smooth rotation of the record carrier. It also addresses methods for starting, stopping or regulating such carriers that are not simple filamentary or web forms.
[G11B 19/2036] Motors characterized by fluid-dynamic bearings -- This subclass covers motors that employ fluid-dynamic bearings to drive or control record carriers in information-storage devices where the carrier moves relative to a transducer. The fluid-dynamic bearings provide low friction and precise positioning, suitable for non-filamentary or non-web media.
[G11B 19/2045] Hubs -- G11B 19/2045 "Hubs" covers components that drive, start or stop rotating record carriers such as disks, but not filamentary tapes or webs, and includes the mechanisms that connect the carrier to a motor or other drive source. It is part of the physics-based information storage subclass dealing with relative motion between the carrier and transducer. This code classifies hub assemblies used in rotary data-storage devices.
[G11B 19/2054] Spindle motor power-up sequences -- G11B 19/2054 pertains to the control of spindle motors that rotate a record carrier, such as a hard-disk platter, during the power-up phase. It covers the timing, sequencing and electrical procedures used to start, accelerate and lock the motor before data recording or retrieval begins. This subclass is applied to devices where the carrier is not a filamentary or web form.
[G11B 19/2063] Spindle motor power-down sequences -- G11B 19/2063 covers the control and timing of power-down sequences for spindle motors that rotate record carriers such as disks, focusing on the procedures that safely reduce speed and stop the motor while preserving data integrity and preventing mechanical damage. It falls under the physics-based information-storage category dealing with relative movement between a record carrier and its transducer.
[G11B 19/2072] for the reduction of power consumption during idle time -- This subclass covers methods and devices that lower the energy used by record-carrier drives when no data is being written or read, such as standby or idle-mode power-saving controls for magnetic or optical storage systems. It belongs to the hierarchy dealing with information storage based on relative movement between the carrier and transducer.
[G11B 19/2081] emergency power-down -- G11B 19/2081 covers mechanisms that provide an emergency power-down to stop or start record carriers that are not filamentary or web forms, such as rotating disks or other media, within devices where information is stored by relative motion between the carrier and a transducer. This falls under the physics-information storage subclass.
[G11B 19/209] in multiple disk arrays, e.g. spindle synchronisation in RAID systems -- The code G11B 19/209 covers methods for controlling the rotation of several disk drives in a storage array, such as synchronising spindles in RAID systems to achieve coordinated operation and data integrity. It falls under physics, information storage, and the movement of record carriers, specifically addressing driving, starting and stopping of non-filamentary carriers.
[G11B 19/22] Brakes other than speed-regulating brakes -- The subclass G11B 19/22 covers braking mechanisms applied to moving record carriers that are not intended to regulate speed, such as devices that stop, hold, or start the carrier after a stop. It includes mechanical, electromagnetic, pneumatic or other brakes used on reels, drums or similar carriers where the braking function is distinct from speed-control systems.
[G11B 19/24] Arrangements for providing constant relative speed between record carrier and head -- G11B 19/24 covers mechanisms that keep a record carrier moving at a constant speed relative to the read/write head in information-storage devices, excluding filamentary or web-type media. It includes arrangements for driving, starting and stopping such carriers while maintaining steady relative motion.
[G11B 19/247] using electrical means -- G11B 19/247 covers the use of electrical means to drive, start or stop record carriers in information-storage devices when the carrier is not a filamentary or web form, nor a support thereof.
[G11B 19/253] using mechanical means -- G11B 19/253 covers the use of mechanical means for driving, starting, or stopping record carriers that are not filamentary or web forms, such as disks or other non-web carriers, in information storage devices where data is recorded by relative motion between the carrier and transducer.
[G11B 19/26] Speed-changing arrangements -- G11B 19/26 covers mechanisms that vary the speed of a record carrier-such as rotating disks or drums-during operation, including devices for accelerating, decelerating, starting, or stopping the carrier where it is not a filament or continuous web. These arrangements are used in information-storage systems that rely on relative motion between the carrier and a transducer.
[G11B 19/265] Friction wheel drive -- G11B 19/265 covers mechanisms that use a friction wheel to drive, start or stop a record carrier in information storage devices where the carrier is not a filament or web. The drive applies relative motion between the carrier and transducer, typically for rotating disks or similar media.
[G11B 19/27] Belt drive -- The G11B 19/27 entry covers physics-based information-storage devices that move a record carrier by means of a belt mechanism rather than filamentary or web forms. It includes the design and control of belt drives that start, stop, or regulate the carrier's motion for data recording or retrieval.
[G11B 19/275] Gear wheel drive -- G11B 19/275 covers gear-wheel drive mechanisms used to start, drive or stop record carriers in information-storage devices where the carrier moves relative to the transducer, excluding filamentary or web-type carriers. It pertains to systems that employ gear wheels to transmit motion to the medium, providing precise positioning and speed control.
[G11B 19/28] Speed controlling, regulating, or indicating -- The CPC class G11B 19/28 deals with speed-controlling, regulating, or indicating devices for record carriers in information-storage systems where the carrier is moved relative to a transducer, excluding filamentary or web forms. It covers mechanisms that start, stop or adjust the carrier's motion such as drives, brakes and speed sensors.
[G11B 20/00] Signal processing not specific to the method of recording or reproducing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  any kind of signal processing which is performed when reading data from or recording data to record carriers. This signal processing specifically includes analogue and digital filtering, equalisation, carrier and symbol synchronization (adjustment of read/write clocks), and the corresponding ways of assessing and improving the quality of the recorded/reproduced signal. Modulation and demodulation techniques (i.e. the actual codes and the stochastical methods for recovering the bit sequences that are reproduced from a record carrier), in the context of recording and reproducing. Techniques of applying error correcting codes in recording / reproducing devices, and likewise how interleaving techniques can be used to mitigate the effects of local burst errors. Techniques for actually detecting media errors (e.g. bad sectors), or data structures and algorithms for coping with these errors, e.g. by relocating data from defective sectors to non-defective spare sectors. The sub-group G11B20/12 also covers the actual format of the record carriers (in the sense of how different kinds of data are arranged on the medium, e.g. documents which describe dedicated areas for storing specific kinds of user or control data, or documents which relate to the data structure of individual sectors). G11B20/00086 is a prominent sub-group, which comprises documents about all sorts of copy protection and digital rights management for record carriers. Since recent copy protection initiatives address the copyright protection issue with techniques which apply likewise to all kinds of different storage media, this sub-group nowadays also includes copyright protection for record carriers which do not necessarily involve any physical movement between a head and the medium.  **Glossary:** - Linear replacement: defect management by relocating the data of defective sectors to a separate spare area - Slipping algorithm: defect management by shifting the beginning of the user area, at the expense of the primary spare area, so as to compensate for defective sectors listed in the PDL. Each defective sector will be replaced by the first good sector following the defective sector. - Skip replacement: defective sectors are skipped; data recording continues at a subsequent good sector - Pre-pit: pre-recorded address pattern on a recordable optical disc - Wobble: radially oscillating pattern of the recording track of an optical disc
[G11B 20/00007] Time or data compression or expansion  audio compression based on psychoacoustics  G10L19/00 ; data processing for reproducing audio data at different playback speeds  G10L21/04 ; video compression   H04N19/00 ; data compression  per se   H03M7/30 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Data compression in the context of recording, both for A/V signals (ATRAC, MP3 etc) and for digital signals in general, e.g. subband coding, transform coding. Also analogue compression, e.g. "time compression/expansion" by altering the density at which the data are recorded, e.g. on an analog tape).
[G11B 20/00086] Circuits for prevention of unauthorised reproduction or copying, e.g. piracy  indicating unauthorised use of record carriers in general  G11B23/28 ; scrambling for television signal recording  H04N5/913 ; network architectures or network protocols for network security  H04L63/00 ; cryptographic mechanisms or cryptographic arrangements for secret or secure communication  H04L9/00 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Copy protection for record carriers; preventing unauthorised access to recorded data; providing means for recognising unauthorised use of data or for distinguishing between authorised and illicit copies; tracing back users, recording devices, or media manufacturers; encryption, decryption, and scrambling algorithms; distributing, updating or revoking encryption keys; secure content acquisition and transmission for recording contents on record carriers; limiting access to a content to certain conditions (certain duration, geographical region, restricted set of users or devices, restricted number of copies, reduced quality). For both digital and analog recording.
[G11B 20/00094] involving measures which result in a restriction to authorised record carriers -- The class covers techniques that impose access or usage restrictions on specific authorised media in storage systems where a record carrier moves relative to a transducer, such as copy-protection, encryption or carrier-specific authentication applied at the signal-processing stage rather than in the recording method.
[G11B 20/00101] the original record carrier having a larger recording capacity than the potential target medium -- The subclass covers cases where the original record carrier stores more information than the target medium can accommodate, so the signal-processing methods address data reduction, compression or selective transfer to fit the smaller capacity. It applies to storage systems based on relative movement between carrier and transducer and is not limited to a particular recording or reproducing technique.
[G11B 20/00108] wherein original, non-rewritable record carriers are recognised by trying to erase recorded data -- The code covers signal-processing methods that identify original, non-rewritable record carriers by attempting to erase the recorded data and detecting the failure to do so. This technique is used to authenticate or verify media that cannot be overwritten, independent of the specific recording or playback process.
[G11B 20/00115] wherein the record carrier stores a unique medium identifier -- This subclass covers signal-processing features that assign or embed a unique identifier on the storage medium itself, allowing the carrier to be distinguished from others regardless of the recording or playback technique. It applies to systems where the medium moves relative to a transducer, such as magnetic tapes or optical discs, and the identifier is stored as part of the medium's data structure.
[G11B 20/00123] the record carrier being identified by recognising some of its unique characteristics, e.g. a unique defect pattern serving as a physical signature of the record carrier -- In this subclass the record carrier is identified by detecting inherent physical features, such as a distinctive defect pattern that acts as a unique signature, rather than by the recording or playback method. The approach falls under signal processing for information storage based on relative movement.
[G11B 20/0013] wherein the measure concerns not the entire record carrier, but a specific physical or logical area of one or more record carriers -- G11B 20/0013 pertains to signal processing techniques in information storage systems where measures are applied specifically to certain areas of a record carrier, rather than to the whole. This classification is used for technologies that target particular physical or logical sections during recording or reproducing processes.
[G11B 20/00137] involving measures which result in a restriction to contents recorded on or reproduced from a record carrier to authorised users -- The subclass covers technologies that impose access or usage restrictions on data stored on a moving record carrier, ensuring that only authorized users can read or reproduce the contents. It includes digital rights management, copy-prevention, encryption and authentication methods applied to magnetic, optical or other media where the carrier moves relative to the transducer.
[G11B 20/00144] involving a user identifier, e.g. a unique customer ID -- It covers signal-processing techniques for information-storage systems that use a user identifier, such as a unique customer ID, to address or retrieve data, without detailing the recording or reproducing method. The subclass sits under physics, information storage based on relative movement, and signal processing not specific to the recording or reproducing process.
[G11B 20/00152] involving a password -- The CPC subclass G11B 20/00152 covers signal-processing techniques applied to storage media that use a password for security, such as magnetic or optical disks where access or operation is controlled by a user-entered code. It concerns the handling of password-related functions in the data path rather than the physical recording or playback mechanisms.
[G11B 20/00159] Parental control systems -- G11B 20/00159 covers parental control systems that manage or limit user access to recorded or reproduced information on storage devices with moving media, using signal-processing techniques that are independent of the specific recording or playback method. These systems may include content filtering, usage restrictions, or authentication functions designed to protect minors.
[G11B 20/00166] involving measures which result in a restriction to authorised contents recorded on or reproduced from a record carrier, e.g. music or software -- G11B 20/00166 covers techniques that enforce access control on data stored on a physical medium, such as digital rights management for music or software. It includes any signal-processing measures that limit playback or copying to authorized users, without addressing the underlying recording or reproduction method.
[G11B 20/00173] wherein the origin of the content is checked, e.g. determining whether the content has originally been retrieved from a legal disc copy or another trusted source -- The subclass deals with signal-processing techniques that verify the origin of content stored on moving-record carriers, such as checking whether data on a disc originates from a legal copy or another trusted source, without being tied to a specific recording or reproduction method.
[G11B 20/00181] using a content identifier, e.g. an international standard recording code [ISRC] or a digital object identifier [DOI] -- The subclass covers techniques that use a content identifier such as an International Standard Recording Code or a Digital Object Identifier to label, track, or retrieve stored information, without being tied to any particular recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00188] involving measures which result in a restriction to authorised devices recording or reproducing contents to/from a record carrier -- The subclass covers techniques that embed or enforce restrictions so that only authorised devices may record or reproduce data on a moving record carrier, such as copy-protection or access-control measures applied during signal processing. It falls under information storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer, specifically signal processing not tied to the recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00195] using a device identifier associated with the player or recorder, e.g. serial numbers of playback apparatuses or MAC addresses -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry G11B 20/00195 pertains to signal processing techniques in information storage systems where data is managed based on the unique identifier of the device used for playing or recording, such as serial numbers or MAC addresses. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with signal processing methods that are not specific to recording or reproducing processes.
[G11B 20/00202] wherein the copy protection scheme builds on multi-session recording, e.g. defective table of contents [TOC] in the 2nd session -- G11B 20/00202 covers signal-processing techniques for copy-protection schemes that exploit multi-session recording on movable-record-carrier media, for example by introducing a defective table-of-contents in a second session to hinder unauthorized duplication. The classification applies to protection methods rather than to the underlying recording or playback processes.
[G11B 20/0021] involving encryption or decryption of contents recorded on or reproduced from a record carrier -- The G11B 20/0021 subclass covers signal-processing techniques that encrypt or decrypt information stored on, or retrieved from, a record carrier, without regard to the specific recording or reproduction method. It applies to any physical data-storage system where the contents are protected by cryptographic transformations.
[G11B 20/00217] the cryptographic key used for encryption and/or decryption of contents recorded on or reproduced from the record carrier being read from a specific source  key distribution or management  H04L9/08 -- This classification code pertains to methods for managing cryptographic keys in the context of information storage, specifically addressing how keys are distributed or managed when encrypting or decrypting contents on a record carrier. It is part of a broader category dealing with signal processing techniques that are not specific to recording or reproducing methods.

[G11B 20/00246] wherein the key is obtained from a local device, e.g. device key initially stored by the player or by the recorder -- The subclass covers signal-processing methods for storage media where the decryption key is sourced from a locally stored device key, such as one pre-installed in a player or recorder, rather than from an external source, enabling secure playback or recording.
[G11B 20/00253] wherein the key is stored on the record carrier -- The subclass deals with data-storage techniques in which an encryption or access key is physically placed on the same record carrier that holds the information, rather than being kept separately. It concerns signal-processing features that are independent of the specific recording or reproducing method, applying to devices where the carrier moves relative to the transducer, such as magnetic or optical discs.
[G11B 20/0026] the key being stored as a barcode -- This subclass covers devices that encode a data-key in a barcode format for reading by an optical or similar transducer, enabling identification, authentication or access control within information-storage systems that rely on relative movement between the carrier and the sensor. The focus is on the barcode key representation rather than the recording or playback technique.

It relates to signal-processing aspects that are not tied to the specific method of recording or reproducing the stored information.

[G11B 20/00282] the key being stored in the content area, e.g. program area, data area or user area  key stored in a management area  G11B20/00297 -- This subclass covers techniques that embed a cryptographic key within the content area of a storage medium-such as a program, data, or user area-or place the key in a dedicated management area, enabling the key to be accessed as part of the stored information. It focuses on key storage inside the carrier rather than on the recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00289] wherein the key is stored as a watermark -- The subclass G11B 20/00289 covers signal-processing techniques that embed a cryptographic key as a watermark within stored information, enabling authentication or copy-protection without being tied to a specific recording or playback method. [G11B 20/00304] the key being stored in the lead-in area [LIA] -- The G11B 20/00304 subclass covers signal-processing aspects of information-storage devices where a cryptographic key or identification data is stored in the lead-in area of a medium, such as the pre-recorded zone that precedes the user data. [G11B 20/00318] the key being stored in the TOC -- The subclass covers techniques that embed a key-such as a cryptographic or identification key-within the table-of-contents area of a storage medium whose record carrier moves relative to a transducer. [G11B 20/00333] the key being stored in header data, e.g. in sector headers -- The entry covers methods that embed a cryptographic key within header information of a storage medium, such as sector headers on magnetic or optical disks, allowing the key to be retrieved during read operations. This approach relates to signal-processing aspects of information storage where the key is stored in the header data rather than in the user payload.

[G11B 20/00369] wherein a first key, which is usually stored on a hidden channel, e.g. in the lead-in of a BD-R, unlocks a key locker containing a second -- This subclass covers techniques where a first key stored in a hidden channel such as the lead-in area of a BD-R disc unlocks a key locker containing a second key, focusing on the signal-processing aspects of key management rather than the recording or reproducing process.
[G11B 20/00376] the key being stored by varying the pit format, e.g. depth, width, length or edge positions -- The code G11B 20/00376 covers methods of storing a cryptographic key by modifying the physical geometry of pits on a recording medium, such as varying their depth, width, length or edge positions, rather than by changing the signal processing itself. It relates to information-storage technologies that use relative movement between the carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00384] the key being derived from a physical signature of the record carrier, e.g. unique feature set -- G11B 20/00384 covers techniques that derive cryptographic keys from the inherent physical signature of a record carrier-such as unique surface features, material variations or micro-structures-when the carrier moves relative to a transducer. [G11B 20/00405] the key being stored by varying characteristics of the recording track, e.g. by altering the track pitch or by modulating the wobble track -- This subclass deals with methods of embedding a security key within an optical or magnetic recording medium by altering physical attributes of the track, such as changing the track pitch or modulating a wobble track, without being tied to a specific recording or playback technique.

[G11B 20/00427] advanced access content system [AACS] -- The G11B 20/00427 entry relates to the Advanced Access Content System, a digital-rights-management framework used to protect high-definition video and audio on optical discs. [G11B 20/00434] content protection for pre-recorded media [CPPM] -- G11B 20/00434 covers technologies for protecting the content of pre-recorded media, such as copy-prevention or encryption mechanisms applied to discs, tapes or other recorded formats. [G11B 20/00442] content protection for recordable media [CPRM] -- G11B 20/00442 deals with content protection for recordable media, commonly referred to as CPRM. [G11B 20/00449] content scrambling system [CSS] -- Content scrambling system (CSS) refers to a signal-processing technique that obscures or encrypts data stored on magnetic or optical media, protecting the information from unauthorized access without affecting the recording or playback mechanisms. It belongs to the class of storage-related processing methods that manipulate the data stream rather than the physical recording process.
[G11B 20/00456] digital transmission content protection [DTCP] -- The entry covers techniques for securing digital media when it is transmitted between devices, focusing on encryption, authentication and key-management methods that prevent unauthorized copying or use.
