[G11B 20/00471] video content protection system [VCPS] -- A video content protection system (VCPS) implements signal-processing techniques to secure video data during storage, transmission or playback, typically using encryption, authentication or copy-control measures that are independent of the specific recording or reproducing method. The classification covers any arrangement that detects, prevents or manages unauthorized access or copying of video streams, often integrating watermarking, conditional access modules or digital rights management functions.
[G11B 20/00478] wherein contents are decrypted and re-encrypted with a different key when being copied from/to a record carrier -- G11B 20/00478 refers to a signal-processing step in magnetic or other movable-head storage systems where data is decrypted during a copy operation and then re-encrypted with a new key before being written to or read from the record carrier. This function is independent of the specific recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00485] characterised by a specific kind of data which is encrypted and recorded on and/or reproduced from the record carrier -- G11B 20/00485 pertains to signal processing in information storage systems, focusing on the encryption and recording of specific types of encrypted data onto, or reproduction from, a record carrier. This classification addresses the handling of encrypted content within the broader context of data storage technologies.
[G11B 20/00492] wherein content or user data is encrypted -- G11B 20/00492 pertains to signal-processing functions that encrypt the content or user data in storage systems whose operation relies on relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer, such as magnetic or optical disks, without being tied to a particular recording or reproduction technique.
[G11B 20/005] wherein only some specific parts of the content are encrypted, e.g. encryption limited to I-frames -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry G11B 20/005 pertains to signal processing techniques in information storage systems where relative movement occurs between the record carrier and transducer. [G11B 20/00507] wherein consecutive physical data units of the record carrier are encrypted with separate encryption keys, e.g. the key changes on a cluster or sector basis -- The entry addresses methods in which each successive physical data unit on a record carrier-such as a cluster or sector-is encrypted with its own distinct key, causing the encryption key to change from one unit to the next and providing per-unit cryptographic protection during storage or retrieval.
[G11B 20/00514] wherein the entire content is encrypted with the same key, e.g. disc key or master key -- G11B 20/00514 falls under physics-information storage based on relative movement, covering signal-processing features that encrypt the whole recorded content with a single key, such as a disc key or master key, rather than using separate keys for individual data blocks. This classification pertains to full-content encryption schemes in movable-media storage.
[G11B 20/00521] wherein each session of a multisession recording medium is encrypted with a separate encryption key -- The G11B 20/00521 subclass concerns signal-processing features for storage devices where the record carrier moves relative to the transducer, specifically the practice of encrypting each session on a multisession medium with its own distinct encryption key.
[G11B 20/00528] wherein each title is encrypted with a separate encryption key for each title, e.g. title key for movie, song or data file -- G11B 20/00528 covers methods where the title of each stored item, such as a movie, song or data file, is encrypted with a separate encryption key for that title, providing individual protection of metadata on an information-storage medium. This falls under signal-processing techniques that are not tied to the specific recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00536] wherein encrypted content data is subjected to a further, iterated encryption, e.g. interwoven encryption -- The G11B 20/00536 classification concerns information storage devices where encrypted data undergoes an additional, repeated encryption step, such as interwoven encryption, to enhance security. It falls under signal-processing techniques for storage media that rely on relative movement between the record carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00543] wherein external data is encrypted, e.g. for secure communication with an external device or for encrypting content on a separate record carrier -- The CPC class G11B 20/00543 addresses signal-processing functions that encrypt data exchanged with an external device or stored on a separate carrier, providing secure communication or content protection independent of any particular recording or reproducing technique. It resides under physics → information storage → storage based on relative movement → signal processing not specific to the method of recording or reproducing.
[G11B 20/0055] wherein license data is encrypted -- G11B 20/0055 relates to signal-processing functions in information storage devices that employ relative motion between a record carrier and a transducer, specifically covering the encryption of license data. The code is applied when the encryption is independent of the recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00557] wherein further management data is encrypted, e.g. sector headers, TOC or the lead-in or lead-out areas -- This CPC subclass covers devices or methods where management information in moving-record storage media-such as sector headers, tables of contents, or lead-in and lead-out zones-is encrypted to protect the data. It falls under G11B 20/00557 within the physics-information storage hierarchy, addressing signal processing that secures auxiliary metadata rather than the primary recorded content.
[G11B 20/00565] wherein parity data is encrypted -- The subclass deals with storage systems that use relative motion between a record carrier and a transducer and includes signal-processing functions not tied to the recording or playback method. It specifically covers techniques in which parity or error-checking data is encrypted, protecting the integrity and confidentiality of the redundancy information used for error detection or correction.
[G11B 20/00572] involving measures which change the format of the recording medium -- G11B 20/00572 covers techniques that modify the physical or logical format of a recording medium, such as altering track layout, data density, or encoding schemes, without addressing the specific recording or playback method. These measures are used to improve storage efficiency, compatibility, or performance in devices where the medium moves relative to the read/write transducer.
[G11B 20/00579] said format change concerning the data encoding, e.g., modulation schemes violating run-length constraints, causing excessive DC content, or involving uncommon codewords or sync patterns -- G11B 20/00579 covers alterations to data formats that affect encoding, such as modulation methods that breach run-length limits, generate excessive direct-current components, or employ unusual codewords or synchronization patterns. It deals with signal-processing aspects not tied to specific recording or playback techniques, focusing on how the encoded signal is structured.
[G11B 20/00586] said format change concerning the physical format of the recording medium -- The G11B 20/00586 classification covers signal-processing features that deal with altering the physical format of a recording medium, such as changing dimensions, track layout, or surface structure, without being tied to a particular recording or playback method. It applies to information-storage systems where the medium moves relative to the transducer.
[G11B 20/00594] wherein the shape of recording marks is altered, e.g. the depth, width, or length of pits -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry G11B 20/00594 pertains to methods in information storage where the physical shape of recording marks, such as their depth, width, or length, is modified during the recording process to enhance data storage or retrieval. This classification is specific to signal processing techniques that are not tied to particular recording or reproducing methods.
[G11B 20/00601] wherein properties of tracks are altered, e.g., by changing the wobble pattern or the track pitch, or by adding interruptions or eccentricity -- This CPC subclass covers modifications to the physical characteristics of recording tracks in magnetic or optical media, such as varying wobble patterns, track pitch, adding interruptions or eccentricity, and is used to encode additional information or improve performance.
[G11B 20/00608] wherein the material that the record carrier is made of is altered, e.g. adding reactive dyes that alter the optical properties of a disc after prolonged exposure to light or air -- This subclass deals with modifications to the record carrier material that cause its optical characteristics to change over time, such as the inclusion of light- or air-reactive dyes that affect disc performance. It concerns signal processing aspects that are independent of how the data are written or read.
[G11B 20/00615] said format change concerning the logical format of the recording medium, e.g. the structure of sectors, blocks, or frames -- This CPC subclass deals with modifications to the logical layout of a recording medium, such as altering sector, block or frame structures, during or after data recording. It covers format changes that affect how data is organized on the medium without addressing the physical recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00623] wherein the modification to the logical format directly concerns user data -- G11B 20/00623 covers signal-processing operations in magnetic or optical storage where the logical format is altered in a way that directly changes the user data, without being tied to a specific recording or playback technique. It falls under physics-based information storage that relies on relative movement between the medium and the transducer.
[G11B 20/0063] wherein the modification to the logical format mainly concerns management data, e.g., by changing the format of the TOC or the subcode -- The subclass covers signal-processing changes that modify the logical format of stored data primarily for management purposes, such as altering a table-of-contents structure or subcode information, while the physical recording method remains unchanged. It applies to storage systems where the carrier moves relative to the transducer, like magnetic tapes or disks, and focuses on managing metadata rather than the recorded content itself.
[G11B 20/00637] said management data being address data -- G11B 20/00637 pertains to the processing of management information that defines the address of data within a storage device, such as control signals that specify where data are stored or retrieved on a moving record carrier. It addresses handling of address data independent of the specific recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00644] the address data format being such that there are overlapping address ranges -- The subclass concerns signal-processing schemes in which the format of address data is defined so that address ranges can overlap, allowing multiple logical blocks to share parts of the address space. This is used in moving-record-carrier storage devices such as tape or disk where the transducer's position relative to the medium is a key factor.
[G11B 20/00652] the address data being scrambled so that ascending address values do not reflect the physical order of data blocks -- The G11B 20/00652 entry covers techniques that scramble address data so that sequential address values no longer correspond to the physical order of data blocks on a storage medium, masking the logical layout from the actual layout. It falls within signal-processing methods for information storage based on relative movement between the record carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00659] involving a control step which is implemented as an executable file stored on the record carrier -- This subclass covers information-storage devices where a processing step is performed by an executable program stored on the same record carrier as the data. The executable file controls functions such as indexing, authentication, or management without being part of the recording or playback method itself.
[G11B 20/00666] involving a step of erasing or nullifying data, e.g. data being overwritten with a random string -- G11B 20/00666 falls under physics-information storage-relative movement between record carrier and transducer and covers signal-processing steps that erase or nullify stored data, such as overwriting information with random strings to render the original content unrecoverable.
[G11B 20/00673] wherein the erased or nullified data include a cryptographic key -- G11B 20/00673 covers signal-processing techniques applied to storage media where data are erased or nullified and that erased data includes a cryptographic key. The classification falls under information storage based on relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer, but it is not tied to any specific recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00681] involving measures which prevent a specific kind of data access -- G11B 20/00681 covers signal-processing techniques that incorporate security features designed to block particular unauthorized data accesses, such as encryption or access-control mechanisms applied to storage devices.
[G11B 20/00688] said measures preventing that a usable copy of recorded data can be made on another medium -- This subclass covers signal-processing techniques that implement copy-prevention or copy-control functions for media where data is read by relative motion between a record carrier and a transducer, such as magnetic tapes or optical disks. It includes encryption, watermarking, key-based access control and other measures that make it impractical to produce a usable duplicate on another medium.
[G11B 20/00695] said measures preventing that data are read from the recording medium -- Measures that inhibit or block unauthorized reading of data stored on a moving record carrier such as magnetic tapes or optical discs are covered by this classification. It includes security techniques - encryption, data scrambling, physical barriers or access control - applied at the signal processing level, independent of the specific recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00702] said measures preventing that data are recorded on the recording medium -- G11B 20/00702 covers measures that prevent data from being recorded on a storage medium in systems where a moving record carrier interacts with a transducer, focusing on signal-processing techniques that are not tied to the specific recording or reproducing method. It addresses safeguards against unintended data imprinting.
[G11B 20/0071] involving a purchase action -- G11B 20/0071 covers signal-processing functions linked to a purchase action in information-storage devices that use relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer, without specifying the method of recording or reproducing.
[G11B 20/00717] wherein accounting and payment are postponed, e.g. until the player can establish a network connection to the service provider -- Code G11B 20/00717 relates to information storage devices that delay accounting and payment until a network connection to the service provider can be established, such as in gaming or media playback systems. The classification covers mechanisms that postpone billing until connectivity is verified.
[G11B 20/00724] wherein a prepaid credit balance is registered on the recording medium -- The subclass G11B 20/00724 covers recording media that store a prepaid credit balance on the medium itself. It falls under signal processing not specific to the recording or reproducing method, within the hierarchy of physics - information storage - information storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00731] involving a digital rights management system for enforcing a usage restriction -- G11B 20/00731 relates to signal-processing functions that embed or manage digital-rights-management (DRM) controls for enforcing usage restrictions on information stored on moving media such as tapes or discs, without specifying the particular recording or playback technique. The classification applies to devices that handle relative motion between a record carrier and a transducer while applying DRM-based access or usage limits.
[G11B 20/00739] wherein the usage restriction is associated with a specific geographical region -- A subclass dealing with location-based usage restrictions applied to information-storage devices or media, where the limitation is tied to a specific geographical region. It covers signal-processing measures that enforce regional access control, independent of the recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00746] wherein the usage restriction can be expressed as a specific number -- This subclass covers signal-processing features of storage devices that involve defining a usage restriction by a specific numeric value, such as a limit on reads, writes, or operating time, independent of the particular recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00753] wherein the usage restriction limits the number of copies that can be made, e.g. CGMS, SCMS, or CCI flags -- This subclass covers digital audio or media systems that embed copy-control information such as CGMS, SCMS, or CCI flags to restrict the number of copies that may be made from a recording. It relates to signal-processing techniques that enforce usage restrictions rather than the physical recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/0076] wherein the copy frequency, i.e. the number of copies in a given time period, is limited -- G11B 20/0076 covers control of copy frequency, meaning a device or system limits the number of reproductions that can be made within a specified time interval. This restriction applies to information storage or retrieval equipment and relates to signal-processing functions that manage how often copies are produced, without specifying the recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00768] wherein copy control information is used, e.g. for indicating whether a content may be copied freely, no more, once, or never, by setting CGMS, SCMS, or CCI flags -- The code covers signal-processing functions in storage media that embed copy-control data such as CGMS, SCMS or CCI flags to indicate whether a content may be copied freely, limited, once, or never.
[G11B 20/00775] wherein said copy control information is encoded in an encryption mode indicator [EMI] -- This subclass deals with signal-processing features in storage devices where copy-control data is conveyed by embedding an encryption-mode indicator within the recorded signal, rather than by a specific recording or reproducing method. It covers the encoding of such control information in the data stream of media that rely on relative movement between carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00782] wherein the usage restriction limits the number of times a program can be installed -- G11B 20/00782 covers signal-processing features that impose usage restrictions limiting the number of times a program may be installed on a storage medium, independent of the recording or reproduction method. It belongs to the physics field under information storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00789] wherein the usage restriction limits the number of functional copies, which can be accessed at a time, e.g. electronic bookshelf concept, virtual library, video rentals or check-in/check out -- This subclass covers systems that impose limits on how many functional copies of a digital item may be accessed simultaneously, such as electronic bookshelves, virtual libraries, video-rental platforms or check-in/check-out mechanisms. The focus is on controlling concurrent usage rather than the recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00797] wherein the usage restriction limits the number of times a content can be reproduced, e.g. using playback counters -- The entry G11B 20/00797 covers digital-content usage controls that limit how many times a piece of media may be reproduced, such as playback-counter mechanisms that enforce a predefined number of plays. It falls under signal-processing features not tied to a specific recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00804] wherein the usage restriction limits the number of users or devices that are allowed to access a given content -- G11B 20/00804 covers signal-processing methods that enforce usage restrictions by limiting the number of users or devices that may access particular digital content. It applies to access-control techniques in information-storage systems that are independent of specific recording or reproducing processes.
[G11B 20/00811] wherein said number is encoded as a cryptographic token or ticket -- This classification code, G11B 20/00811, pertains to the encoding of numbers as cryptographic tokens or tickets in signal processing for information storage systems. It falls under the broader categories of physics and information storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00818] wherein the usage restriction limits the signal quality, e.g. by low-pass filtering of audio signals or by reducing the resolution of video signals -- The G11B 20/00818 subclass covers methods that deliberately degrade signal quality as a usage restriction, such as applying low-pass filtering to audio or lowering the resolution of video. It pertains to information-storage devices where signal processing is not tied to the recording or reproducing technique.
[G11B 20/00826] wherein a spoiler signal is added to degrade the signal quality -- G11B 20/00826 relates to signal-processing methods in devices where a spoiler signal is intentionally added to the data stream to degrade signal quality, for purposes such as copy protection or security in relative-movement information-storage systems.
[G11B 20/00833] wherein the usage restriction limits the data access speed, e.g. by defining a maximum bit rate of the I/O interface -- G11B 20/00833 covers signal-processing features that impose usage restrictions on data storage devices by limiting the data-access speed, such as defining a maximum bit-rate for the input-output interface. It applies to systems where information is stored by relative motion between the carrier and transducer, without being tied to a specific recording or reproduction method.
[G11B 20/0084] wherein the usage restriction can be expressed as a specific time or date -- G11B 20/0084 covers usage restrictions on information storage devices that are expressed as a specific time or date, independent of the recording or reproducing method. It belongs to the physics group for information storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer, focusing on signal processing not tied to the recording technique.
[G11B 20/00847] wherein the usage restriction is defined by a licence file -- The subclass covers signal-processing arrangements that control data use through a licence file, specifying usage restrictions irrespective of the underlying recording or playback mechanism. It applies to storage systems where a moving record carrier and transducer are used, such as magnetic or optical media, and the restriction is implemented in software rather than in the physical format.
[G11B 20/00855] involving a step of exchanging information with a remote server -- The CPC subclass G11B 20/00855 concerns signal-processing functions that are not tied to the specific recording or playback method, but that involve exchanging data with a remote server. It covers any step where an information-storage device or transducer communicates over a network to send, receive, or synchronize information with a distant computing system.
[G11B 20/00862] wherein the remote server can grant the permission to use a content -- G11B 20/00862 covers information-storage systems in which a remote server controls access by granting permission to use digital content, focusing on the access-control functionality rather than the physical recording or playback technique. It falls under physics → information storage → storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer → signal processing not specific to recording or reproducing.
[G11B 20/00869] wherein the remote server can deliver the content to a receiving device -- G11B 20/00869 relates to signal-processing techniques for delivering digital content from a remote server to a receiving device, independent of the specific recording or playback method. It covers the handling, formatting and transmission of data streams to enable remote access of stored information.
[G11B 20/00876] wherein physical copy protection means are attached to the medium, e.g. holograms, sensors, or additional semiconductor circuitry -- This CPC subclass covers physical copy-protection devices attached to a storage medium, such as holograms, sensors or additional semiconductor circuitry, to deter unauthorized duplication. It belongs to signal processing not specific to the method of recording or reproducing within information storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00884] involving a watermark, i.e. a barely perceptible transformation of the original data which can nevertheless be recognised by an algorithm -- G11B 20/00884 covers the use of digital watermarks, i.e. barely perceptible alterations of stored data that can be detected by an algorithm. It applies to information storage media where data is read by relative movement between carrier and transducer, without specifying the recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00891] embedded in audio data -- This subclass covers techniques that hide or embed supplemental information within an audio waveform using signal-processing methods that are independent of the recording or reproduction mechanism. It includes audio steganography, watermarking and other forms of data insertion for copy-protection, metadata or auxiliary data transmission.
[G11B 20/00898] based on a hash function -- G11B 20/00898 covers signal processing techniques that use hash functions in information storage systems where data is accessed by relative movement between carrier and transducer, without specifying recording or reproducing methods.
[G11B 20/00905] multiple watermarks used in combination -- The G11B 20/00905 entry covers signal-processing techniques that employ several watermarks simultaneously to enhance data authentication or copy protection. It applies to information-storage devices where a record carrier moves relative to a transducer, such as magnetic or optical media, and the method is independent of the specific recording or playback process.
[G11B 20/00913] based on a spread spectrum technique -- This subclass covers signal processing methods that employ spread spectrum techniques for information storage systems where data is read or written by relative motion between a carrier and a transducer, without being tied to a particular recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/0092] involving measures which are linked to media defects or read/write errors -- This subclass covers signal-processing techniques that detect, compensate for or otherwise handle defects in the storage medium or read/write errors, without being tied to a particular recording or playback method. It applies to magnetic, optical or other moving media where the carrier moves relative to the transducer.
[G11B 20/00927] wherein said defects or errors are generated on purpose, e.g. intended scratches -- The subclass deals with deliberately creating defects such as scratches on a storage medium to serve as markers, copy-protection features or authentication cues, rather than as accidental errors. These purposeful imperfections are processed as part of the signal handling but are not tied to the actual recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/00934] said intentional errors occurring because of corrupted address information -- G11B 20/00934 covers signal-processing techniques that handle intentional errors caused by corrupted address information in storage systems where data are accessed by relative movement between the carrier and the transducer, without being tied to any particular recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00942] said intentional errors occurring due to an invalid playback path or program chain -- This group covers signal-processing techniques related to intentional errors that arise when a playback path or program chain is invalid, in systems where a record carrier moves relative to the transducer, such as magnetic tape or optical disc playback.
[G11B 20/00949] said intentional errors occurring due to bad sectors, which are either physically destroyed or which are declared defective in the defect management information -- G11B 20/00949 relates to signal-processing techniques for information storage devices where data are recorded by relative motion between a carrier and a transducer, specifically addressing intentional errors that arise from bad sectors that are physically damaged or flagged as defective in defect-management data.
[G11B 20/00956] said intentional errors occurring due to an invalid TOC -- G11B 20/00956 covers techniques that deliberately introduce errors into recorded data when a table of contents is detected as invalid, typically to prevent unauthorized use or to signal tampering. It is a signal-processing method for storage media that rely on relative movement between the carrier and transducer.
[G11B 20/00963] wherein said defects or errors are not generated on purpose, e.g. random defect patterns occurring during the normal manufacture -- This subclass deals with detection, analysis, or handling of unintended, random defects and errors that arise naturally during manufacturing of moving-record media rather than intentional error patterns. It covers signal-processing techniques for identifying such spontaneous defects in storage systems where the carrier moves relative to the read/write head.
[G11B 20/00971] involving measures for monitoring the industrial media production and distribution channels, e.g. for controlling content providers or the official manufacturers or replicators of recording media -- This subclass covers signal-processing methods used to monitor and control industrial production and distribution of recorded media, such as overseeing content providers, manufacturers, or replication facilities, independent of any particular recording or playback technique.
[G11B 20/00978] wherein the record carrier stores a trial version of a content -- G11B 20/00978 defines a subclass of information-storage devices in which the record carrier holds a trial-preview version of the content, enabling limited access or evaluation before full acquisition. It belongs to the physics field under information storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer and concerns signal processing not tied to the recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/00985] the trial version being of lower quality than the original version -- This classification code, G11B 20/00985, pertains to signal processing techniques where a trial version of content is intentionally made of lower quality compared to the original version. This is often used for preview or promotional purposes in digital media storage systems.
[G11B 20/00992] Circuits for stereophonic or quadraphonic recording or reproducing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Recording multichannel signals, e.g., stereo or quadraphonic signals, but also if more than 2 or 4 channels are involved.
[G11B 20/02] Analogue recording or reproducing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Analogue recording or reproducing, e.g. audio cassettes, grammophone records, laser discs etc. A further refinement of this subgroup addresses error detection and correction ( G11B20/025 ), direct recording or reproducing ( G11B20/04 ), recording and reproducing angle-modulated signals ( G11B20/06 , mostly FM modulated audio signals), recording and reproducing pulse-modulated signals (e.g. FM audio in video tapes).
[G11B 20/025] Error detection or correction -- G11B 20/025 covers techniques for detecting and correcting errors in data streams used in magnetic or optical storage systems where the carrier moves relative to the read/write transducer. It includes coding schemes, parity checks, and algorithms that improve reliability without being tied to a specific recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/04] Direct recording or reproducing -- G11B 20/04 covers technologies that directly record or reproduce information by converting signals into a physical form on a moving carrier or retrieving them, without specifying the particular recording or reproducing method, within the realm of information storage based on relative movement between carrier and transducer.
[G11B 2005/0002] Special dispositions or recording techniques -- G11B 2005/0002 covers inventions that implement special dispositions or recording techniques for storing or reproducing information, including unusual media handling, unique data encoding, and non-standard methods of imprinting or retrieving recorded content. It pertains to devices or processes that go beyond conventional recording by employing distinctive arrangements, formats, or operational steps to achieve the recording function.
[G11B 2005/0005] Arrangements, methods or circuits -- G11B 2005/0005 concerns digital data processing and covers the overall architecture, operational procedures and circuit designs used in computers and other electronic devices that manipulate binary information. The entry includes hardware arrangements, processing methods and circuit implementations that enable storage, retrieval, transformation or transmission of digital data.
[G11B 2005/0008] Magnetic conditionning of heads, e.g. biasing -- G11B covers magnetic recording and reproducing devices, with the 2005 group focusing on magnetic heads. Sub-entry 0008 describes techniques for conditioning heads magnetically, such as applying bias currents or fields to optimize their performance and reduce distortion. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO to index patents on head biasing methods.
[G11B 2005/001] Controlling recording characteristics of record carriers or transducing characteristics of transducers by means not being part of their structure -- The G11B 2005/001 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers methods for adjusting the recording or transducing characteristics of media or devices by applying external influences such as magnetic fields, electric signals or optical illumination, rather than altering the physical structure of the carrier or transducer.
[G11B 2005/0013] of transducers, e.g. linearisation, equalisation -- G11B 2005/0013 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to techniques and methods for improving the performance of transducers, such as linearization or equalization, which are crucial for enhancing signal fidelity and efficiency in data storage systems.
[G11B 2005/0016] of magnetoresistive transducers -- The G11B 2005/0016 subclass pertains to devices that convert magnetic field variations into electrical signals using the magnetoresistive effect. It includes transducers such as read/write heads for magnetic storage, position sensors and other components that exploit changes in resistance of magnetic materials.
[G11B 2005/0018] by current biasing control or regulation -- G11B 2005/0018 covers recording or reproducing devices that employ a bias current to control or regulate the magnetic or electrostatic medium, such as tape, disc or card heads. The classification applies to systems where a low-level current is applied to improve linearity, reduce distortion or set operating thresholds during signal recording or playback.
[G11B 2005/0021] Thermally assisted recording using an auxiliary energy source for heating the recording layer locally to assist the magnetization reversal -- G11B 2005/0021 covers magnetic recording methods that employ a secondary energy source, such as a laser or resistive heater, to locally raise the temperature of the recording layer. The heating reduces coercivity, allowing magnetization reversal with a lower magnetic field. This principle is used in heat-assisted magnetic recording technologies.
[G11B 2005/0024] Microwave assisted recording -- G11B 2005/0024 covers recording methods and apparatus that employ microwave energy to assist the writing, erasing or modification of information on a medium. The microwaves may be used to locally heat or alter magnetic, optical or other storage layers, improving data density or recording speed. Applications include microwave-assisted magnetic recording and similar technologies.
[G11B 2005/0026] Pulse recording -- G11B covers information storage and recording technologies, and the subgroup 2005 is directed to specific recording methods. Code 2005/0026 pertains to pulse recording, which includes techniques that store data by generating and detecting pulses-such as magnetic, optical or electronic pulses-to represent binary information on a medium.
[G11B 2005/0029] using magnetisation components of the recording layer disposed mainly perpendicularly to the record carrier surface -- The entry refers to magnetic recording techniques in which the magnetisation of the recording layer is oriented mainly perpendicular to the surface of the record carrier. This configuration, known as perpendicular magnetic recording, is employed to increase storage density in devices such as hard-disk drives and magnetic tapes. It falls under CPC class G11B, which covers various recording arrangements and magnetic recording media.
[G11B 2005/0032] Transducing means or record carriers including or interacting with each other through interposition of, a physically controllable magnetic flux masking or focusing element -- A device in which a transducer or storage medium interacts with another through a magnetically controllable element that masks or focuses the magnetic flux, allowing the coupling between them to be adjusted physically. This includes magnetic recording heads, read/write assemblies and related components that employ a movable or variable magnetic flux guide.
[G11B 2005/0034] switchable at least locally between two different physical states, e.g. magnetic and non-magnetic -- The subclass G11B 2005/0034 covers recording or data-handling devices that contain elements capable of being switched locally between two distinct physical states, such as magnetic and non-magnetic, to modify their function or data storage properties. It is used for inventions where the state change is confined to a limited region of the component, allowing selective control of magnetic behavior within the device.
[G11B 2005/0037] using superconductive elements -- The G11B 2005/0037 entry concerns information-storage devices that incorporate superconductive materials, such as superconducting write heads, read heads, or magnetic-field generators. It covers methods and apparatuses that use the low-loss, high-current properties of superconductors to improve recording density, signal-to-noise ratio, or operating speed in magnetic storage systems.
[G11B 2005/00843] allowing digital compact cassette [DCC] format recording -- G11B 2005/00843 covers inventions that enable recording using the digital compact cassette (DCC) format, including methods, apparatuses and control systems for encoding digital audio onto magnetic tape according to the DCC standard. It falls under the broader class G11B for recording and reproducing technologies.
[G11B 2005/3996] large or giant magnetoresistive effects [GMR], e.g. as generated in spin-valve [SV] devices -- The CPC class G11B 2005/3996 covers technologies that exploit large or giant magnetoresistive effects, such as those observed in spin-valve devices, for reading or writing magnetic information or sensing magnetic fields. It includes devices and methods that use GMR to convert magnetic changes into electrical signals.
[G11B 20/06] Angle-modulation recording or reproducing -- G11B 20/06 covers angle-modulation techniques used for recording or reproducing information where a carrier moves relative to a transducer. It pertains to signal-processing methods that modulate the angle of a carrier wave, rather than the amplitude, without specifying the particular recording or playback mechanism. This sub-class lies under information storage based on relative movement between the record carrier and transducer.
[G11B 2007/0003] Recording, reproducing or erasing systems characterised by the structure or type of the carrier -- G11B 2007/0003 covers recording, reproducing or erasing systems that are defined primarily by the physical structure or type of the information carrier, such as magnetic, optical, or solid-state media. The classification emphasizes the carrier's architecture and material characteristics rather than data format or processing method.
[G11B 2007/0006] adapted for scanning different types of carrier, e.g. CD & DVD -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code G11B 2007/0006 pertains to technology designed for reading or writing data on various types of optical storage media, such as CDs and DVDs, with a focus on systems that can adapt to different carrier formats.
[G11B 2007/0009] for carriers having data stored in three dimensions, e.g. volume storage -- G11B 2007/0009 classifies carriers in which data are stored throughout a three-dimensional volume rather than on a surface, covering technologies such as holographic memory, multilayer optical discs and other volumetric storage media that exploit depth to increase capacity.
[G11B 2007/0013] for carriers having multiple discrete layers -- The code refers to recording or reproducing devices that use a carrier made of several separate layers, each capable of holding information. It includes multi-layer optical discs, stacked magnetic media and similar multi-layer storage substrates.
[G11B 2007/0016] for carriers adapted to have label information written on the non-data side by the optical head used for data recording, e.g. lightscribe, labelflash -- G11B 2007/0016 covers optical recording carriers that are built to allow a label to be printed on their non-data side using the same optical head that records data, exemplified by technologies such as LightScribe and LabelFlash. It pertains to media designed for simultaneous data storage and laser-etched labeling.
[G11B 2007/00457] Two photon recording -- G11B 2007/00457 covers the use of two-photon recording techniques for storing or processing information, where a material is altered by simultaneous absorption of two photons to create a latent image or data pattern. The method is employed in high-density optical storage, micro-fabrication and three-dimensional data encoding.
[G11B 2007/00653] Collinear holography -- The CPC subclass G11B 2007/00653 covers holographic techniques in which the reference and object beams propagate along the same axis, a method known as collinear holography. It includes the recording, processing and reconstruction of holograms that use a single optical path to simplify alignment and improve stability.
[G11B 2007/00656] Counterpropagating holography -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/00656 covers counterpropagating holography, a technique for recording holograms in which two coherent light beams travel in opposite directions through the recording medium. It includes methods, apparatuses and materials that generate, process or read such holograms, typically for data storage, imaging or security applications.
[G11B 2007/00709] Dimensions of grooves or tracks, e.g. groove depth, track pitch -- G11B 2007/00709 refers to the dimensional characteristics of recording grooves or tracks, including parameters such as groove depth, track width, and track pitch, as applied to information storage media like magnetic, optical or other patterned surfaces. This sub-group falls within the broader G11B class covering recording and reproducing devices.
[G11B 2007/00727] where the information is modified to form a visible pattern, e.g. forming a label by modifying the width of pits or grooves -- The code covers methods for altering recorded information so that it creates a visible pattern, such as producing a label by varying the width of pits or grooves on a medium. It relates to techniques that modify the physical form of stored data to generate recognizable visual markings.
[G11B 2007/00754] Track shape, e.g. address or synchronisation information in wobbled track or sidewall -- The classification G11B 2007/00754 relates to the design of a recording medium's track shape to carry address or synchronization information, for example by using a wobbled track, sidewall modulation or other intentional geometric variations.
[G11B 2007/00763] Track cross-section, e.g. V-shaped, trapezoidal -- G11B 2007/00763 covers the shape of the track that guides moving elements, such as the V-shaped or trapezoidal cross-section used in printers, scanners, or other document-handling apparatus. The classification addresses the geometry that influences friction, stability and alignment of the feed mechanism.
[G11B 2007/0133] Details of discrete information structures, e.g. shape or dimensions of pits, prepits -- This CPC subgroup pertains to the characteristics of individual physical features used to encode data on a medium, such as the geometry, size or arrangement of pits and pre-pits that represent digital information. It covers the specification, measurement or modification of these discrete structures in recording and reading processes.
[G11B 2007/0136] where each location can have more than two values ('multivalue'), for data or prepits -- G11B 2007/0136 covers storage techniques in which each physical location-such as a cell, spot or pit-can represent more than two distinct values, enabling multivalued data encoding or pre-pit patterns for higher information density. This includes methods and devices that exploit such multilevel states to store digital information.
[G11B 2007/0919] Critical angle methods -- G11B 2007/0919 covers methods for determining or using the critical angle in optical reading or scanning devices, such as measuring the angle at which total internal reflection occurs to read or write data.
[G11B 2007/092] Dither methods -- G11B 2007/092 covers dither methods, which are techniques that add controlled noise to a signal or image before quantisation or halftoning in order to minimise visible artefacts such as banding. The entry includes algorithms, pattern generation and processing steps used in printing and digital imaging to improve perceived tonal smoothness.
[G11B 2007/0922] Far-field methods -- G11B 2007/0922 covers optical techniques that acquire or record information at distances where the light propagates in the far field, typically many wavelengths away from the target surface. It includes methods such as far-field scanning, projection and imaging used in document readers, barcode scanners and similar devices.
[G11B 2007/0924] Skewed beams methods (using an angled beam, i.e. a beam which is reflected from the disc at an angle different from 90°) -- G11B 2007/0924 relates to optical information storage where the read/write beam is reflected from the disc at an angle other than 90°, i.e., a skewed or inclined beam, and covers methods and apparatus that use such angled beams to improve access, signal quality or reduce interference.
[G11B 2007/13722] Fresnel lenses -- G11B 2007/13722 covers Fresnel lenses, which are flat or stepped-profile lenses that focus or redirect light with a series of concentric grooves. The classification is used for devices such as printers, scanners, projectors, and other optical systems that employ these compact, lightweight focusing elements.
[G11B 2007/13725] Catadioptric lenses, i.e. having at least one internal reflective surface -- Catadioptric lenses combine refractive and reflective optics, incorporating at least one internal mirrored surface to bend light alongside conventional lens elements. This design purposefully merges lenses and mirrors to correct aberrations, reduce size, or increase focal length in cameras, telescopes, and other imaging devices.
[G11B 2007/13727] Compound lenses, i.e. two or more lenses co-operating to perform a function, e.g. compound objective lens including a solid immersion lens, positive and negative lenses either bonded together or with adjustable spacing -- The code G11B 2007/13727 covers optical assemblies that consist of two or more lenses working together to achieve a specific function. It includes compound objective lenses, such as those that incorporate a solid-immersion lens, and configurations where positive and negative elements are either bonded or spaced with adjustable distance.
[G11B 2007/240004] characterised by the form of the carrier -- G11B 2007/240004 relates to data-storage or recording devices that are distinguished mainly by the shape or geometry of the carrier medium, such as a particular form of tape, disc, card or other substrate, rather than by its material composition or magnetic properties.
[G11B 2007/240008] Cards -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/240008 pertains to cards used for storing and processing data, including magnetic-stripe, chip, smart and similar cards employed for identification, access control, payment, or other information-carrying functions. It covers the design, manufacture, reading, writing and security aspects of such card-based media.
[G11B 2007/240012] intended for rotation -- G11B covers information storage, retrieval, reading or writing, and the 2007/240012 subgroup specifies devices whose recording medium is intended for rotation, such as magnetic or optical disks, drums or cylinders. This classification is used by both the EPO and USPTO to categorise rotating-media storage technologies.
[G11B 2007/240017] Tapes -- G11B 2007/240017 covers tapes used as recording media for storing information, including magnetic or optical formats employed in audio, video, data backup and computer storage applications. It pertains to the physical tape medium and its structure rather than the recording or playback devices.
[G11B 2007/240021] Cylinders -- Cylinders in CPC G11B 2007/240021 refers to storage or recording elements having a cylindrical shape, such as magnetic or optical cylinders used for data retention. The classification covers devices, methods and processes for producing, handling, and reading or writing information on these cylindrical media.
[G11B 2007/240025] for storing optical interference patterns, e.g. holograms -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Volumetric holographic storage -> G11B2007/0009 - Holographic carriers in the form of a card or other rectangular shape, if not invention information -> G11B7/24F2 , G11B2007/240008 - Holographic tape carriers, if not invention information -> G11B7/24F4 , G11B2007/240017
[G11B 2007/24302] Metals or metalloids -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code G11B 2007/24302, used by the European and United States patent offices, categorizes patents involving metals or metalloids in the context of magnetic recording materials.
[G11B 2007/24304] group 2 or 12 elements (e.g. Be, Ca, Mg, Zn, Cd) -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/24304 covers inventions that use elements from group 2 or group 12-such as beryllium, calcium, magnesium, zinc or cadmium-in recording, printing or related photographic processes. It groups patents where these metals serve as catalysts, coatings, sensitizers or other functional components in image-forming technologies.
[G11B 2007/24306] transition metal elements of groups 3-10 -- G11B 2007/24306 classifies inventions that make use of transition metal elements belonging to groups 3-10 of the periodic table, including their preparation, alloys, or specific functions in devices such as magnetic media or recording components. The entry falls within the G11B section for information storage and retrieval technologies.
[G11B 2007/24308] transition metal elements of group 11 (Cu, Ag, Au) -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry G11B 2007/24308 pertains to the use of transition metal elements from group 11, specifically copper (Cu), silver (Ag), and gold (Au), in magnetic recording media.
[G11B 2007/2431] group 13 elements (B, Al, Ga, In) -- The CPC subclass G11B 2007/2431 groups patents that involve the group 13 elements-boron, aluminium, gallium and indium-in optical-recording technologies or related photonic applications, covering materials, alloys, compounds and processes that use these elements for media, lasers or other information-storage devices.
[G11B 2007/24312] group 14 elements (e.g. Si, Ge, Sn) -- G11B 2007/24312 denotes a CPC group covering inventions that employ group-14 elements-silicon, germanium, tin and related materials-in semiconductor components or processes for data processing and recording devices. The classification is used for patents addressing the material properties, fabrication methods, or applications of these elements in electronic hardware.
[G11B 2007/24314] group 15 elements (e.g. Sb, Bi) -- G11B covers printing processes and related equipment; the sub-group 2007/24314 specifies the use of group-15 elements, notably antimony (Sb) and bismuth (Bi), in such technologies. This includes their application in inks, pigments, coatings or conductive layers that improve print quality or functionality.
[G11B 2007/24316] group 16 elements (i.e. chalcogenides, Se, Te) -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/24316 covers the use of group-16 elements, notably selenium and tellurium, as active materials in electro-optical or phase-change memory devices, where reversible amorphous-to-crystalline transitions provide data storage. It belongs to the broader class for information storage based on electro-optic effects.
[G11B 2007/24318] Non-metallic elements -- G11B 2007/24318 classifies inventions that employ non-metallic elements-such as carbon, silicon, nitrogen or oxygen-in the composition of printing plates, inks, recording media, or other devices used for storing, presenting, or reproducing information. It is a sub-group of G11B, which covers technologies for information handling and printing.
[G11B 2007/2432] Oxygen -- G11B covers information storage systems that rely on the relative or absolute position of a recording medium, such as magnetic, optical, or electro-chemical media. The 2007/2432 entry focuses on the role of oxygen in these technologies, for example its use in oxidation processes to form or modify recording layers, to improve data stability, or to enable specific write-or-read mechanisms. This subclass groups patents that address how controlled oxygen exposure affects storage performance.
[G11B 2007/24322] Nitrogen -- G11B 2007/24322 is a CPC subclass that addresses the use of nitrogen in information-storage technologies, including nitrogen-filled chambers, gas-filled optical media, or nitrogen-controlled environments for semiconductor or magnetic memory devices. It falls under G11B, which covers storage, retrieval and processing of data.
[G11B 2007/24324] Sulfur -- The entry G11B 2007/24324 in the Cooperative Patent Classification, used by the European and United States patent offices, pertains to sulfur in the context of magnetic or optical recording media. This classification covers specific aspects related to the use of sulfur in these materials.
[G11B 2007/24326] Halides (F, CI, Br...) -- This CPC subclass concerns halide compounds such as fluorides, chlorides, bromides and related elements used in the formulation, application or treatment of information-recording media. It covers their role in photographic, printing or other data-storage technologies.
[G11B 2007/24328] Carbon -- G11B 2007/24328 covers optical recording media that incorporate carbon-based materials, such as carbon inks, carbon black particles or carbon nanostructures, as recording layers, reflectors or additives to enhance data density, contrast, thermal stability or durability of the stored information.
[G11B 2007/2445] containing an azulene compound -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/2445 relates to information-storage or recording media that incorporate an azulene compound, such as dyes, inks, or coatings used to impart specific optical or coloration properties. It covers any device, substrate or layer where the presence of an azulene derivative is a functional feature of the recording or display function.
[G11B 2007/24606] Azo- dyes -- G11B 2007/24606 pertains to azo-dyes employed in printing, writing and recording media. It covers the chemical structure, synthesis and formulation of these organic colourants for use in inks, toners, pigments and other marking substances, focusing on their optical properties and performance in image-forming applications.
[G11B 2007/24612] two or more dyes in one layer -- G11B 2007/24612 covers printing processes in which two or more different dyes are applied together in a single layer to create a multicolour image, rather than depositing separate layers for each colour. The classification is used for technologies that mix dyes before or during deposition to achieve combined hues in one pass.
[G11B 2007/24618] two or more dyes in two or more different layers, e.g. one dye absorbing at 405 nm in layer one and a different dye absorbing at 650 nm in layer two -- G11B 2007/24618 covers multilayer optical data carriers that incorporate two or more distinct dye layers, each tuned to absorb different wavelengths such as a 405 nm dye in the first layer and a 650 nm dye in the second layer. The arrangement enables multiplexed reading or encoding of information by exploiting separate spectral regions.
[G11B 2007/24624] fluorescent dyes -- This CPC subclass covers fluorescent dyes employed in printing inks and related media, particularly for marking, security features, or optical brightening. It falls under G11B 2007, which addresses colourants and pigments used in printing processes.
[G11B 2007/24705] Cyanine -- Cyanine refers to a class of organic dyes used as sensitizing agents in electrophotographic photoconductors and related imaging layers. In the CPC, it falls under G11B 2007/24705, covering chemical compounds that enhance charge generation or light-absorption properties of recording media. These cyanine compounds are employed to improve the efficiency and spectral response of photocopying and laser-printing processes.
[G11B 2007/2471] Merocyanine -- Merocyanine refers to a class of photo-responsive dyes that undergo a reversible colour change when exposed to light or an electric field, making them suitable for use in recording, printing and electro-chromic display technologies covered by CPC G11B. This entry groups patents on merocyanine-based materials, processes and devices for image formation and information storage.
[G11B 2007/24715] Oxonol -- G11B 2007/24715 refers to the use of oxonol compounds as charge-transport or electrochromic agents in recording, printing or display media, particularly in electrophotographic or electrostatic processes. The entry falls under the CPC group for data writing and printing technologies that employ organic dyes for image formation.
[G11B 2007/24905] neutral -- G11B 2007/24905 falls under the CPC class for information storage and retrieval and specifically covers neutral recording media or methods, i.e., media that store data without relying on magnetic, electrostatic or optical polarity. It includes devices, materials, or processes that use a neutral state to represent bits, enabling non-volatile storage with reduced sensitivity to external fields.
[G11B 2007/2491] as anion -- G11B 2007/2491 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to magnetic recording materials where the active component is specified as an anion, indicating a focus on the chemical nature of the material used for data storage.
[G11B 2007/24915] as cation -- G11B 2007/24915 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to a specific aspect of magnetic recording, particularly concerning the use of cations in the composition or treatment of recording media. This classification is used by both the European and United States patent offices to organize and search patents related to this technology.
[G11B 2007/25301] comprising glass -- G11B 2007/25301 designates optical information-storage devices or media that contain glass, for example glass discs, plates or substrates used to record, read or retrieve data by reflecting, transmitting or diffracting light.
[G11B 2007/25302] comprising metals -- G11B 2007/25302 - comprising metals - designates magnetic recording media whose magnetic layer includes metal particles, alloys or metal-based compounds, using the metal component to achieve specific coercivity, remanence or signal-to-noise performance.
[G11B 2007/25303] comprising resins -- G11B 2007/25303 covers paper-handling or information-recording devices and processes that use media comprising resins, such as resin-coated paper, synthetic paper or other recording sheets where a resin binder forms part of the substrate. The entry includes the manufacture, treatment, and use of such resin-based media in printing, copying, or data-storage applications.
[G11B 2007/25304] Polycarbonate [PC] -- The CPC code G11B 2007/25304 designates the use of polycarbonate (PC) as a material in recording and data-storage media, especially as the substrate for optical discs and similar devices. It covers the manufacturing, structural features and functional properties of PC that enable reliable information recording and retrieval.
[G11B 2007/25305] Polyester, e.g. PET, PETG, PEN -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/25305 covers the use of polyester substrates such as polyethylene terephthalate, PETG and poly(ethylene naphthalate) as carriers or base layers in recording media and related information-storage devices. It includes the preparation, treatment and application of these polymers for recording, printing, optical or magnetic data formats.
[G11B 2007/25306] Polystyrene [PS] -- G11B 2007/25306 refers to the use of polystyrene as a substrate or recording layer in optical storage media, such as compact discs and DVDs. It classifies inventions involving polystyrene-based materials for data storage, where information is recorded by altering the material's optical properties. This entry falls under the broader G11B group covering recording media formed by deformation of a material.
[G11B 2007/25307] Polycycloolefines [COCs] -- G11B 2007/25307 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers polycycloolefins, polymeric materials with several cycloalkene rings incorporated into their backbone, which are employed primarily as coating compositions and in related printing or information-storage technologies.
[G11B 2007/25308] Biodegradable polymers, cellulose included -- G11B 2007/25308 designates patents concerning biodegradable polymers, particularly those comprising or derived from cellulose. The subclass covers the formulation, processing and applications of such polymers for environmentally friendly materials, including packaging, films and fibers.
[G11B 2007/25402] consisting essentially of organic resins -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/25402 groups inventions that use components made essentially of organic resins in fixing, marking or coding processes. It applies to devices, methods or compositions where the resin forms the primary material for creating permanent or temporary marks on a substrate.
[G11B 2007/25405] comprising inorganic filler, e.g. particles, fibres -- The G11B 2007/25405 subgroup relates to paper, board or similar sheet materials that incorporate inorganic fillers such as particles or fibres. It covers compositions, processes or treated products where the filler is added to improve properties like strength, opacity, surface smoothness or printability. This classification is used for inventions describing the inclusion of mineral or synthetic fillers within the pulp, coating or additive layers of paper-based items.
[G11B 2007/25408] consisting essentially of inorganic materials -- This CPC entry groups inventions in the field of information storage that are characterized by a composition that is essentially inorganic. It covers recording media, storage devices or related processes where the functional layer or substrate consists mainly of inorganic materials such as ceramics, metal oxides or glass. The classification is applied when the inorganic nature of the material is central to the disclosed technical solution.
[G11B 2007/25411] containing transition metal elements (Zn, Fe, Co, Ni, Pt) -- G11B 2007/25411 classifies recording or reproducing media and devices that incorporate transition-metal elements such as zinc, iron, cobalt, nickel or platinum, usually as magnetic particles, conductive layers or catalytic components in inks or photosensitive layers. It is used to identify compositions that enhance magnetic, electrical or chemical properties of recording materials.
[G11B 2007/25414] containing Group 13 elements (B, Al, Ga) -- The CPC subclass G11B 2007/25414 groups inventions whose material composition includes elements from Group 13 of the periodic table-specifically boron, aluminum or gallium. It is used for patents involving sheets, films or other solid media where these elements are integral to the functional properties, such as conductivity, hardness or light-weight characteristics.
[G11B 2007/25417] containing Group 14 elements (C, Si, Ge, Sn) -- The G11B 2007/25417 subclass groups patents for information-storage devices or media whose functional layers incorporate Group 14 elements-carbon, silicon, germanium or tin-either as the primary material of the recording surface, the substrate, or a key component of the storage structure.
[G11B 2007/25701] consisting essentially of organic materials -- G11B 2007/25701 classifies recording or storage media whose substrate is formed mainly of organic materials, such as paper, cellulose, polymer films or other naturally-derived or synthetic organic substances. The code is used for inventions that rely on these organic media for capturing, retaining or presenting information.
[G11B 2007/25703] Resins -- G11B 2007/25703 falls under CPC class G11B for printing technologies and designates inventions relating to resin compositions used in printing, such as inks, plates, or print media, and their preparation or modification to improve performance.
[G11B 2007/25705] consisting essentially of inorganic materials -- G11B 2007/25705 covers information storage or recording media whose functional layer is made primarily of inorganic substances such as glass, ceramics, metal oxides or other mineral compounds. The code applies to devices, methods and products that rely on the physical or chemical properties of these inorganic materials rather than organic polymers.
[G11B 2007/25706] containing transition metal elements (Zn, Fe, Co, Ni, Pt) -- G11B 2007/25706 classifies inventions concerning recording or storage media that incorporate transition-metal elements such as zinc, iron, cobalt, nickel or platinum, usually to enhance magnetic, optical or chemical characteristics of the medium. It applies to compositions, layers or additives where these metals are integral to the medium's functional performance.
[G11B 2007/25708] containing group 13 elements (B, Al, Ga) -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/25708 covers inventions in the field of recording, reproducing or processing information where the device or medium incorporates elements from group 13 of the periodic table-boron, aluminium or gallium. It includes the use of these elements in semiconductors, optical layers or protective coatings for storage media.
[G11B 2007/2571] containing group 14 elements except carbon (Si, Ge, Sn, Pb) -- G11B 2007/2571 covers information storage or recording media that incorporate group-14 elements other than carbon, such as silicon, germanium, tin or lead. The classification applies when these elements are used in the substrate, active layer, or other functional parts of the storage device.
[G11B 2007/25711] containing carbon -- The CPC class G11B 2007/25711 refers to information-storage media that incorporate carbon in their composition, such as paper, carbon-coated foils, carbon-based optical discs or other recording substrates where carbon is a functional element of the carrier. It encompasses devices and methods that use these carbon-containing materials for storing data or images.
[G11B 2007/25713] containing nitrogen -- The CPC entry G11B 2007/25713 pertains to paper-handling or data-processing equipment that uses or processes media containing nitrogen, such as nitrogen-based inks, toners or coatings applied during printing or copying.
[G11B 2007/25715] containing oxygen -- G11B 2007/25715 classifies inventions concerning information-storage devices or recording media where the functional layer includes an oxygen-containing material such as oxides, oxygen-bearing polymers or similar compounds. The presence of oxygen is a defining technical feature that influences the medium's optical, electrical or chemical behavior.
[G11B 2007/25716] containing sulfur -- G11B 2007/25716 refers to printing technologies or materials that incorporate sulfur, such as inks, toners, or plate coatings in which sulfur-containing compounds are used to achieve specific functional or chemical properties. This subgroup falls under the broader CPC class G11B for printing processes and related apparatus.
[G11B 2007/25718] containing halides (F, Cl, Br, l) -- This CPC subgroup includes information-storage or recording media whose composition contains halide compounds such as fluorides, chlorides, bromides or iodides, for example photographic films, magnetic tapes or optical disks that use halide chemistry for data retention or processing.
[G11B 2007/2581] based on aluminium -- G11B 2007/2581 relates to printing, marking or copying processes that use aluminium as the material or substrate for forming characters, symbols or other marks, such as aluminium type, plates or foil employed to create the printed image.
[G11B 2007/2582] based on silver -- The classification G11B 2007/2582 refers to photographic processes that employ silver-based light-sensitive compounds, typically silver halide emulsions, for recording and developing images. It includes methods of preparing, exposing, developing, fixing, or printing such silver-based photographic media.
[G11B 2007/2583] based on gold -- The code G11B 2007/2583 designates patents dealing with optical information-storage media that incorporate gold as a functional material. It covers devices, layers or coatings where a gold film, alloy or nanoparticle structure is used to provide reflectivity, plasmonic enhancement or other recording-readout functions.
[G11B 20/08] Pulse-modulation recording or reproducing -- The G11B 20/08 class covers technologies that use pulse-modulated signals to record or reproduce information, such as converting data into a series of pulses for storage on magnetic, optical or other media and retrieving it by detecting those pulses. This falls under signal-processing methods applied to information storage based on relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer.
[G11B 20/10] Digital recording or reproducing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Digital recording or reproducing. Processing pipeline of a typical recording apparatus: an A/V signal is compressed ( G11B20/00007 ), error correction codes are added ( G11B20/1833 , G11B20/1866 ), the signal is modulated ( G11B20/14 ), equalisers and filters improve the signal quality ( G11B20/10009 ), then the signal is recorded to the record carrier according to a given format ( G11B20/12 ).
[G11B 20/10009] Improvement or modification of read or write signals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Modifying and improving the read or write signals (i.e. removing jitter, increasing the SNR), e.g. by using equalisers and filters; anything about how to adjust the frequency and phase of the read/write clock or the bit clock of the demodulation circuit, e.g. clock adjustment with a PLL; anything related to PRML techniques (Partial Response Maximum Likelihood); A/D conversion, recovering the bit string from the analogue HF signal; maximum likelihood estimation and related techniques for recognising the correct bit sequences, e.g. using the Viterbi algorithm. Wobble detection can also be classified here if the document is linked to clocking.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Code-related aspects of clock adjustment, e.g. documents which describe specific synchronisation patterns -> G11B20/1403 - Specific modulation schemes to be applied to a wobbled pre-groove -> G11B20/1419
[G11B 20/10018] analog processing for digital recording or reproduction  G11B20/10037  -  G11B20/10481  take precedence -- G11B 20/10018 covers analog signal processing techniques used in digital audio or video recording and playback systems, such as equalization, filtering, and level adjustment performed before conversion to digital form or after digital-to-analog conversion. It falls under information storage based on relative movement between record carrier and transducer and is distinct from methods specific to recording or reproducing.
[G11B 20/10027] adjusting the signal strength during recording or reproduction, e.g. variable gain amplifiers  optimum power control for optical discs  G11B7/125 -- G11B 20/10027 covers techniques for adjusting signal strength during the recording or playback of information, such as the use of variable-gain amplifiers and optimum power control mechanisms, particularly applied to optical disc systems. It belongs to the physics field of information storage that relies on relative motion between a record carrier and a transducer.
[G11B 20/10037] A/D conversion, D/A conversion, sampling, slicing and digital quantisation or adjusting parameters thereof -- This CPC class covers signal-processing functions that convert analog signals to digital form or vice versa, such as sampling, slicing, quantisation and the adjustment of related parameters, independent of any particular recording or reproduction method. It is situated in the hierarchy for information storage based on relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer.
[G11B 20/10046] filtering or equalising, e.g. setting the tap weights of an FIR filter -- G11B 20/10046 covers signal-processing techniques used to filter or equalise data streams, such as selecting the tap weights of a finite-impulse-response (FIR) filter, irrespective of the recording or playback method. It falls under physics → information storage → relative-movement storage → signal processing not specific to recording/reproducing.
[G11B 20/10055] using partial response filtering when writing the signal to the medium or reading it therefrom -- This subclass covers the application of partial-response filtering to the write or read signal in a storage system where the record carrier moves relative to the transducer. The technique shapes the signal waveform to control intersymbol interference and enable higher data densities, without being tied to a particular recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/10064] EEPR4 or E2PR4, i.e. extended partial response class 4, polynomial (1-D)*(1+D) 3 -- This CPC subclass covers signal-processing techniques for magnetic or optical data storage in which the recorded waveform follows an extended partial-response class 4, implemented with the polynomial (1-D)(1+D)³. It is used to shape the read-back signal to improve detection and error correction without specifying the recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/10074] EPR4, i.e. extended partial response class 4, polynomial (1-D) *(1+D) 2 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry G11B 20/10074 pertains to signal processing techniques in information storage systems, specifically addressing the use of EPR4, or Extended Partial Response Class 4, which involves a polynomial (1-D)*(1+D)² for enhancing data recovery and error correction.
[G11B 20/10083] PR1 or PR(1,1,), i.e. partial response class 1, polynomial 1+D -- G11B 20/10083 covers the partial-response class 1 signalling method, expressed by the polynomial 1 + D, used in signal-processing for information-storage systems where a record carrier moves relative to the transducer.
[G11B 20/10092] partial response PR(1,1,1,1) -- The G11B 20/10092 entry covers signal-processing techniques that employ a partial-response filter with the PR(1,1,1,1) characteristic, shaping the read-channel response to improve bit detection in information-storage systems. It is not tied to any particular recording or reproducing method, focusing instead on the equalization and detection aspects of the data signal.
[G11B 20/10101] PR2 or PR(1,2,1), i.e. partial response class 2, polynomial (1+D)2=1+2D+D2 -- G11B 20/10101 pertains to signal-processing techniques that employ the partial-response class-2 (PR2) method, defined by the polynomial (1 + D)² = 1 + 2D + D², to shape the recorded waveform in magnetic or optical information-storage systems. This equalization approach controls intersymbol interference by deliberately allowing a controlled amount of pre- and post-cursor distortion, enabling higher data densities while simplifying the detection circuitry.
[G11B 20/10111] partial response PR(1,2,2,1) -- G11B 20/10111 covers signal-processing methods for storage devices that rely on relative movement between a record carrier and a transducer, specifically the partial-response coding scheme PR(1,2,2,1). It includes techniques that shape the recorded waveform to manage intersymbol interference without being tied to a particular recording or playback method.
[G11B 20/1012] partial response PR(1,2,2,2,1) -- G11B 20/1012 covers a partial-response signal-processing technique, specifically the PR(1,2,2,2,1) equalizer, used in information-storage systems where data are read by relative motion between a record carrier and a transducer. It addresses the shaping and equalisation of read-back signals without referring to a particular recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/10129] partial response PR(1,2,3,3,2,1) -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry G11B 20/10129 pertains to a specific type of signal processing technique used in information storage systems. This technique, known as partial response PR(1,2,3,3,2,1), is designed to enhance data retrieval by managing the inter-symbol interference in the read-back signal.
[G11B 20/10138] partial response PR (2,3,3,2) -- This subclass deals with signal-processing methods that apply a partial-response filter with the coefficient pattern 2-3-3-2 to the readout signal in magnetic or optical storage systems, improving detection accuracy without specifying the recording or reproducing method.
[G11B 20/10148] partial response PR(1,3,3,1) -- The entry covers a partial-response signaling scheme identified as PR(1,3,3,1), used in read-back channels for storage devices where a record carrier moves relative to a transducer. It defines the signal-processing characteristics that shape the output waveform to control intersymbol interference and support higher data densities without being tied to a particular recording or playback method.
